Chef Gino D'Acampo gets a rude awakening to play the Midnight Gameshow, and one audience member plays a game of Soap Or Nope - and receives her very own cliffhanger when she meets her pen-pal of 70 years for the very first time.(n)

He is seduced by the glamorous world of high finance and seeks the chance to provide for his family's security - but to get it, he will have to deprive other families of their homes. Drama, starring Andrew Garfield, Michael Shannon and Laura Dern.(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Undercover cameras help to explore the world of animal intelligence, ingenuity and creativity in their hunting, protection and health. Spy Orang-utan meets her real-life counterparts in Borneo to capture how, living close to local people, they have gained unexpected skills in sawing wood and washing with soap, while Spy Termite catches one of the world's cleverest animal tricksters, the drongo, as he tries to outwit a group of meerkats in the battle for food.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: As well as tending to his crops, much of Adam Henson's time on his Cotswold farm is devoted to caring for his animals. Each and every one of them is important to him, from his commercial flock of sheep to his chickens, and from his pigs to his ponies, with many of them having become much-loved characters to the show's viewers. This special edition of Countryfile takes a journey into the archives to revisit and rediscover some of Adam's highs and lows with his marvellous menagerie of creatures.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Crathes Castle, Aberdeenshire, where finds brought in to be valued include a collection of signed Harry Potter books, ornate silver from Iona, a German art deco figurine, an intricate Italian bracelet, and a baseball cap with an unusual story to tell.(n)

programme.title: The Salisbury Poisonings
programme.desc: On March 4, 2018, emergency services descend on Salisbury city centre where they find former spy Sergei Skripal and his daughter Yulia unconscious on a park bench. They are taken to hospital where doctors struggle to diagnose the source of their illness, while DS Nick Bailey decides to investigate the Skripals' home for further casualties, but soon after returning to work he too begins to feel ill. Tracy Daszkiewicz, Director of Public Health for Wiltshire Council, is called into an emergency meeting, where it is revealed that it was an attempted assassination with an unidentified poison. Fact-based drama, starring Rafe Spall and Anne-Marie Duff.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: George Floyd: A Killing That Shook the World - Panorama
programme.desc: The death of George Floyd in Minneapolis has prompted the biggest protests about race and police brutality in the US for 50 years. Around the world, thousands of people have joined marches against racism. Reporter Clive Myrie asks if this could be a moment that changes race relations in America for good. He hears from protesters, eyewitnesses and former police officers about why this killing has had such a powerful impact, and speaks to people from black and white communities in Minneapolis about their hopes and fears for the future.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bobby is devastated when Ian tells him that Peter kissed Dotty, while Linda hears how Phil has been treating Sharon and tells Mick she no longer wants to let him have the Vic. Chantelle tries to clear the air with Gray by proving she was at the dentist when he thought she was using his car - but he still thinks she is lying - and Sonia, feeling lonely, decides to search for her long-lost father.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Steph McGovern, Richard Osman, Jennifer Saunders and Joe Sugg join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies.(n)

programme.title: The Salisbury Poisonings
programme.desc: Tracy Daszkiewicz and Supt Dave Minty watch the bodycam footage of DS Bailey walking round the Skripal house and finally make a breakthrough, when they realise he and the Skripals were contaminated by Novichok placed on the front door of the house. As Nick fights for his life, his wife Sarah is beside herself with worry, but tries to remain calm for their daughters. Fact-based drama, starring Rafe Spall and Anne-Marie Duff.(n)

programme.title: I May Destroy You
programme.desc: The action flashes back to three months before the incident as Terry visits Arabella in Ostia, a district of Rome on the Tyrrhenian Sea, where the up-and-coming author is having a whale of a time - all at the expense of her agent. After procuring supplies from local drug dealer Biagio, the pair have a wild night out that takes an unexpected turn for both of them. Drama exploring the question of sexual consent in contemporary life, created by and starring Michaela Coel, with Weruche Opia and Marouane Zotti.(n)

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer, alongside Maya Jama and Alex Horne, hosts a new variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Plus, an exclusive performance in The Garden Sessions from James Bay.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: History mad Oscar plans to transport his mums back over 400 years to the English Civil War, complete with highway robberies, geese herding and even a rap battle.(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood uncover strange tales, including the unidentified black blob invading the California coast, and a lost underwater world halfway up a mountain.(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie continues his road trip at the World Crazy Golf Championships in Hastings.(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.sub-title: Blue Peter: Six Badges of Summer - Green Week!
programme.desc: Lindsey Russell, Richie Driss and Mwaksy Mudenda reveal all the exciting things viewers can do to get hold of a Green Blue Peter Badge.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall examines the methods animals employ to protect themselves against predators and reveals martial arts experts of the natural world, including a praying mantis and a big-hitting shrimp. Plus, he trains alongside a kung fu expert.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Wildlife expert Steve Backshall examines the increasing pressures threatening the planet's animal populations, including tigers, gorillas and whales. He explores the various conservation efforts set up to help them and reveals a few success stories, such as the remarkable comeback of Africa's White Rhino.(n)

programme.title: Forces of Nature with Brian Cox
programme.desc: The physicist charts the Earth's journey through space, taking to the air in a fighter jet to race the spin of the planet and reverse the passage of the day. In Brazil, a professional surfer rides a monstrous wave surges up the Amazon River, marking the world's constant dance with the Moon, while the harsh winter in Greenland brings opportunity to the Inuit people who live there, and Brian explains how comprehending the motion and speed of Earth brings an understanding of the nature of space and time itself.(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Former Great British Bake Off winner Nadiya Hussain is in East Anglia as she continues her culinary road trip around Britain, going out to sea with Norfolk fisherman Richard Matthews to catch crabs for Vietnamese-style summer rolls. She then heads to Ely in Cambridgeshire to meet potato farmer and crisp factory owner Ross Taylor, using his snacks to make a crisp, chocolate and salted peanut dessert. Back in her kitchen, Nadiya creates a ploughman's cheese and pickle tart, and a herby chicken and potato salad.(n)

programme.title: Planes: Fire & Rescue (2014)
programme.desc: A racing plane suffers damage to his engine, and realises he can never compete again. He starts a new career as a member of a firefighting squadron, which proves even more dangerous than he expected when the aircraft have to deal with a massive forest blaze. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Dane Cook and Ed Harris.(n)

programme.title: The £100K House: The Final Fix
programme.desc: Piers Taylor revisits Wajid and Anam from Burnley, who have hit a problem as they near the completion of their ambitious home. They are struggling to make the largest room feel welcoming, so the presenter searches for an ingenious design solution, delivering a radical idea inspired by a fashionable hotel. Meanwhile, Heidi and Steven's house in north east Scotland still sits on a barren piece of wasteland. Presented with the seemingly impossible task of transforming the garden with a budget of just £1,000, Piers takes a risk and shows the pair a piece of landscape architecture in Edinburgh.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the five remaining contestants take part in the quarter-final, creating garments and trying their hand at techniques from around the world. Firstly, the sewers must follow a pattern like they have never encountered before, as judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask them to make a Filipino Terno Blouse. In the Transformation Challenge, the competitors are asked to turn Provencal tablecloths from France into a brand new outfit in just 90 minutes, before creating a dramatic Flamenco skirt from Southern Spain in the final challenge.(n)

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: Behind-closed-doors action from Bislett Stadium in Oslo, featuring the likes of pole vaulter Armand Duplantie and sprint hurdler Karsten Warholm. With commentary by Steve Cram.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple hoping to move to Lincolnshire on a budget of £500,000, and visits Lincoln cathedral to find out about its conservation plans.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers bike from north to south through Europe, searching for the best baking each country has to offer. They start their 5,000-mile trek in Norway, picking up tips and recipes from professionals and home bakers - including a 95-year-old who still makes waffles on her 65-year-old waffle iron. The duo also rustle up some of their own favourite Norwegian recipes - Scandinavian rye bread, cardamom and lemon cookies, and ham and cheese-filled pastries.(n)

programme.title: Hugh's Wild West
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall explores the Somerset Levels, where he visits Worthy Farm, home to Glastonbury Festival, before heading to the Secret World Rescue Centre.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the Ulster Folk and Transport Museum in Belfast, where he is joined by experts Thomas Plant and Caroline Hawley.(n)

programme.title: Cricket: England v West Indies Greatest Matches
programme.desc: A look back at the memorable First Test of the 1991 series, which came from Headingley. The Windies had won every series between the sides since 1974, but the hosts went into this match with confidence led by Graham Gooch.(n)

programme.title: Inside Central Station
programme.desc: Documentary series following the people that work all hours to keep Glasgow Central Station on track. Thousands of Ed Sheeran fans push the events team to their limit.(n)

programme.title: The Unwanted: The Secret Windrush Files
programme.desc: David Olusoga opens previously unseen government files to argue that the Windrush scandal and the so-called hostile environment for black British immigrants has been 70 years in the making. The film features Sarah O'Connor, Anthony Bryan and Judy Griffith. Settled in the UK legally since childhood, they were re-classified as illegal immigrants by new regulations. Unable to show proof of their nationality status, they lost jobs, savings and their health, facing deportation back to countries they could barely remember.(n)

programme.title: David Bowie: Finding Fame
programme.desc: The final part of Francis Whately's trilogy of films on Bowie's life, telling the story of the five years when many of the ideas that would help him become an icon were first born. Starting in 1966, soon after David Jones changed his name to David Bowie, the film traces his interest in everything from Holst and Pinky and Perky to Anthony Newley and Tibetan Buddhism, and how he used all of these influences to create not only Ziggy Stardust, but the material for his entire career.(n)

programme.title: David Bowie: Five Years
programme.desc: Documentary exploring five key years in the singer's career, which saw him redefine himself as an artist by adopting a different persona - each one marking a shift in musical direction. Featuring interviews with his collaborators, the programme focuses on 1971's ultimate glam-rock icon Ziggy Stardust, Bowie's reinvention as the impeccably dressed soul stylist the Thin White Duke in 1975, his regeneration in Berlin with the critically acclaimed 1977 album Heroes, 1980's Scary Monsters triumph and Let's Dance's global success in 1983.(n)

programme.title: David Bowie: The Last Five Years
programme.desc: A portrait of David Bowie, told by the people who knew him best - his friends and artistic collaborators. It also takes a detailed look at his last albums The Next Day and Blackstar, and his play Lazarus. Through the prism of this last work, the film shows how, in his final five years, Bowie not only began producing music again but returned to the core and defining themes of his career. Among the contributors are Tony Visconti, Gail Ann Dorsey, Toni Basil and Michael C Hall.(n)

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Special edition of the archive hits programme dedicated to David Bowie, featuring performances from Top of the Pops and The Old Grey Whistle Test as well as archive interviews.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode's alphabetical journey through the BBC's culinary archive continues as he reaches the letter N, for which highlights include Nigel Slater's prawn and noodle soup and Raymond Blanc's greengage nougatine.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Steve Brown explores Elmley Nature Reserve on the Isle of Sheppey, where swallows and swifts are gathering in their hordes, heralding the arrival of summer. Margherita Taylor is on the hunt for an invasive species of moth that threatens not just the oaks on London's Hampstead Heath, but walkers too. On his farm, Adam Henson's horses get their hooves checked and he catches up with trainer Jonjo O'Neill to see how his racehorses are coping with life off the track.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don makes space in the writing garden for plants that will flower later in the season and plants out tender vegetables for late summer harvests. Cameras revisit Frances Tophill's allotment to join her as she demonstrates an unusual method of reducing weeds and also catch up with a gardener in Wales who has dedicated years to breeding some very special varieties of rhododendrons. Plus, Joe Swift heads to Devon to find out about the design and planting challenges faced when creating a coastal garden.(n)

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond and Nick Nairn are in their kitchen's cooking with fantastic Scottish produce. Euan McIlwraith delves into the history of his beloved vintage Land Rover, Arlene Stuart explores the River Tweed, while Anne Lundon investigates the impact of Covid-19 on the National Trust for Scotland. Plus, a day in the life of farmer and comedian Jim Smith.(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Brian Cunningham sows and grows his own, while George Anderson takes a pot-luck kerb-side delivery. He also shares a recipe for a rainbow of bedding colour in containers.(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare a selection of extravagant British gourmet dishes, using high-quality ingredients including lobster, truffles, prime cuts of meat and expensive chocolate. The duo also explore the stories behind the recipes.(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Nigel Havers reminisces about his childhood in Suffolk, and regails TV chef Brian Turner with stories of betting at Newmarket at the age of 5 and setting fire to a listed building in his parents' garden, amongst other capers. He also recalls being sent to boarding school and regular canings.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at television appearances made over the years by Oscar-winning Hollywood actor Charlton Heston, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: The Big Country (1958)
programme.desc: A naive sea captain learns a harsh lesson in cowboy lore when he becomes embroiled in a family feud. Tensions are running high between his future father-in-law and a hillbilly clan over the thorny issue of water rights - and neither party is impressed by the newcomer when he refuses to take sides. Western, starring Gregory Peck, Charlton Heston, Jean Simmons, Burl Ives and Carroll Baker.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at the TV appearances made over the years by Oscar-winning American actor Gregory Peck, revealing the milestones of his life and career, which led to him being considered a Hollywood icon. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix narrates a documentary giving an insight into the principality on the French Riviera, with access to the royal palace as well as the super-rich and those who serve them. In the first edition, director Michael Waldman explores how this extraordinary country operates, meeting everyone from Prince Albert and his staff to wealthy widow Monika Bacardi, who takes him on a tour in her chauffeur-driven classic Rolls-Royce. He also goes track side with ex-Formula One driver David Coulthard and team owner Eddie Jordan during the Monaco Grand Prix.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.sub-title: Inside the Factory: Keeping Britain Going
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace catches up with workers at the Walkers Crisps factory in Leicester, which he visited in 2016, finding out how they are coping during the coronavirus crisis. He speaks to manager Carl Lewis, who reveals that in order to keep up with demand they're making big changes to their output, switching away from single packets to a situation where 90 per cent of production is now the multipacks sold in supermarkets.(n)

programme.title: Turkey with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the second half of the journey, Simon travels along the Black Sea Coast to the capital of Ankara and the Northern Highlands, witnessing first hand the aftermath of fighting in Kurdish areas and meeting a conservationist attempting to preserve the region's brown bears. He then returns to Istanbul, where he takes part in a popular TV series set in the palace of the Ottoman Sultans.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den: Best Ever Pitches
programme.desc: Joe Lycett narrates a selection of memorable moments when the pitchers turned the tables on the panel, including the poker game entrepreneur who forced the dragons to compete against each other for his business. Plus, the inventor of a phone app who provoked the wrath of the panellists and a rejected contestant who went on to become a big commercial success.(n)

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show from isolation, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by a selection of celebrity guests.(n)

programme.title: Reggie Yates: Life and Death in Chicago
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels to Chicago to investigate gun crime in President Obama's adopted hometown to find out why so many of the victims are young African-American men. He hears first-hand the accusations of police brutality, but after attending the aftermath of a shooting, and the funeral of a young black man, he comes to realise an even bigger problem is the majority of killings are perpetrated by African-Americans living in the city's poorest neighbourhoods.(n)

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Behind Bars
programme.desc: The journalist goes inside America's notorious, overcrowded San Quentin State Prison and meets some of its 3,000 inmates, including a man serving a 521-year sentence and a transgender couple who live like husband and wife. He also learns how the convicts have created their own world, complete with a brutal code of conduct, and how they cope with the prospect of spending the rest of their days behind bars.(n)

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: The gang deals with rival bikers intruding on their turf, while Galindo wonders if Emily is to blame for the Mayor's misgivings.(n)

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: A biker summit leads to a troubling alliance, which proves costly. Emily makes a damaging discovery while digging into the past for EZ, but is not sure who she can confide in.(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from viewers at home on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week.(n)

programme.title: Life and Birth
programme.desc: Baby Elsie has been born eight weeks premature, weighs just 860 grams and was born with a hole in her heart. Her parents have spent the past two months living in hospital accommodation waiting for her to receive emergency surgery before they can take her home - but the procedure will only be possible when her weight reaches two kilograms. A couple enter the final preparations for their home delivery, and a mother who suffered severe post-natal depression after the birth of her first son gives birth to her third child.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Essie attempts to act as if there is nothing to worry about as she starts chemotherapy, but confides in Fletch that she knows they will both face difficult decisions in the future. Guy's past is dredged up, but gets the chance to prove he is better than his reputation - if he can convince Ric to let him operate. Jac is determined to secure a bright future for Darwin after losing a profitable contract, but fears her feelings for Kian may be a distraction.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Saving Our Nurses
programme.desc: Newly qualified physiotherapist Esther dreads her forthcoming move from the neurology department to outpatients and the proposed switch is causing her some worrying health problems. Occupational therapist Melissa cares deeply for her patients, but her dyslexia and the demands of essential paperwork means she's losing motivation for her job.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The red and blue teams vie at Brighton Racecourse, East Sussex, hunting for items of value with the help of experts Catherine Southon and Mark Stacey. Presenter Christina Trevanion maintains order and also shows off a tasty mystery item.(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Adventures
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull travel around Britain as they explore their favourite scientific breakthroughs, beginning with the 18th-century inventions that kick-started the Industrial Revolution and transformed scientific understanding. They head to Derbyshire to investigate Richard Arkwright's water frame - a spinning machine that allowed cotton textiles to be mass-produced, and then examine James Watt's separate condenser, an unassuming invention that would be the biggest single improvement ever made to the steam engine.(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament presented by Alicia McCarthy.(n)

programme.title: Atlantic: The Wildest Ocean on Earth
programme.desc: Life in the tropical Atlantic, where everything from dolphins, manatees and whale sharks to sunbathing jellyfish thrive in the Caribbean's warm, sheltered waters, fringed by coral reefs and rich mangrove forests. But when extreme heat in Africa unleashes terrifying hurricanes, all hell breaks loose across the region.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Chase the Case
programme.desc: Strategic game show hosted by Dan Walker in which five contestants trade general knowledge for information to gain access to a case containing a secret amount of cash.(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen continue their culinary journey around the nation in Scarborough, North Yorkshire. In their quest to find the best local food, they visit a fish and chip cafe with a glamorous history, go on a train ride and attend a tea-dance with more than 30 different varieties of cake for them to sample.(n)

programme.title: Euros Rewind
programme.desc: Netherlands v France. A chance to see the Euro 2008 Group C encounter at Stade de Suisse in Bern, Switzerland.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and experts Will Axon and Mark Stacey visit Powderham Castle in Devon, where Items brought in by members of the public include a pristine collection of Dinky toys.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a journey from East Yorkshire to Caernarfon, beginning by travelling from the city of Hull to the North Yorkshire market town of Malton.(n)

programme.title: The Planets
programme.sub-title: A Moment in the Sun: The Terrestrial Planets
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox tells the life story of the solar system, beginning with the development of the four rocky worlds closest to the Sun - Mercury, Venus, Earth and Mars. They were born at the same time from the same material - yet have lived radically different lives. What immense forces are at play?(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.sub-title: Inside the Factory: Keeping Britain Going
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace catches up with workers at the Typhoo Tea factory on the Wirral, which he visited back in 2017, where production has been upped to produce 109 million bags a week. In reports from the programme's archives, Cherry Healey visits a tea processing plant in Kenya, and Ruth Goodman sheds light on how Second World War troops risked their lives to brew up.(n)

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: The programme goes behind the scenes as Casino de Monte Carlo holds an exclusive event with a Prohibition theme aimed at high rollers as it seeks to attract new clients, while at the palace Prince Albert II hosts the new French ambassador. In Monaco's only tattoo parlour, Princess Stephanie's daughter Camille Gottlieb is getting a discreet new inking and sharing what life is like for her in the principality, and four billion euros' worth of motor vessels enter the harbour for the annual yacht show.(n)

programme.title: Comedians: Home Alone
programme.desc: Tim Key performs his isolation poetry, Morgana Robinson showcases her impersonation of Cheryl Cole and Charlie Higson brings The Fast Show's Swiss Toni back to life. Plus, Bob Mortimer shows what it is possible to create from a slab of butter, a tin of sardines and an orange.(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Angela Barnes, Ed Gamble, London Hughes, Rhys James and Milton Jones join regular panellist Hugh Dennis on the comedy quiz show, hosted by Dara O Briain. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: A House Through Time
programme.desc: David Olusoga explores the house's history from the 1880s to the First World War, a period that saw the property sliding down the social scale and turning from a family home to a multiple occupancy. He discovers how a resident responded to the death of his baby daughter by opening a milk bar for the temperance movement, only to get into legal trouble for watering down his product. David also discovers the story behind a mysterious occupant who lived in the house under a pseudonym and learns how the battle of Passchendaele changed the residents' lives forever.(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On the Isle of Lewis in the Outer Hebrides, ex-barrister Sandy Granville and his fellow crofters collect their sheep from the moor and club together to dip them in insecticide before mating time. Meanwhile, on the mainland in the west, Sybil and George Macpherson gather their herd of cattle and separate the cows from their calves before they are sent off to lowland farms for the winter.(n)

programme.title: Earth's Natural Wonders
programme.desc: Documentary profiling some of the world's most expansive - and inhospitable - landscapes, and the people who live there. A fisherman in Zambia faces off against crocodiles and risks being swept to his death as he tries to make a living above the imposing Victoria Falls, and in the Amazon, a giant fish offers salvation to a hungry family that calls the river home. A boy's future hangs on the outcome of a single horse race in the vast grasslands of the Steppe, and in a European salt marsh, a young man duels with a savage bull in a centuries-old battle of wills.(n)

programme.title: The Young Montalbano
programme.desc: A man is shot dead in a hotel room, and the ensuing police investigation means Montalbano must spend his first New Year's Day in Vigata taking part in a murder inquiry. All thoughts of festivities are driven from his mind when the case presents him with several conundrums, including the fact that the victim was a co-owner of the establishment in which he died. In Italian.(n)

programme.title: Guys and Dolls (1955)
programme.desc: A gambler accepts a bet that he cannot win the heart of a strait-laced Salvation Army member. Meanwhile, his friend has to find a secret location for an illegal dice game he is organising - while also contending with the police, a mobster and a girlfriend desperate to drag him to the altar. Musical, starring Frank Sinatra, Marlon Brando and Jean Simmons. Songs include Luck Be a Lady Tonight and Sit Down You're Rocking the Boat.(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On the Isle of Lewis in the Outer Hebrides, ex-barrister Sandy Granville and his fellow crofters collect their sheep from the moor and club together to dip them in insecticide before mating time. Meanwhile, on the mainland in the west, Sybil and George Macpherson gather their herd of cattle and separate the cows from their calves before they are sent off to lowland farms for the winter.(n)

programme.title: Earth's Natural Wonders
programme.desc: Documentary profiling some of the world's most expansive - and inhospitable - landscapes, and the people who live there. A fisherman in Zambia faces off against crocodiles and risks being swept to his death as he tries to make a living above the imposing Victoria Falls, and in the Amazon, a giant fish offers salvation to a hungry family that calls the river home. A boy's future hangs on the outcome of a single horse race in the vast grasslands of the Steppe, and in a European salt marsh, a young man duels with a savage bull in a centuries-old battle of wills.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Martyn Colbeck travels to the diverse island of Sri Lanka to study its elephants, a subspecies of the Asian genus that have their own unique characteristics. Drawing on local knowledge, he begins to unravel the complex social world of the creatures and takes an interest in an orphaned animal that lost a leg in a snare and faces an uncertain future.(n)

programme.title: Highlands: Scotland's Wild Heart
programme.sub-title: Autumn and Winter - Against All Odds
programme.desc: Ewan McGregor narrates as summer gives way to autumn and winter in the Highlands. From October to February, extreme weather is expected to batter the Cairngorm plateau. For the region's wildlife, these brutal, dark seasons trigger a game of endurance for which they must carefully prepare. Throughout the autumn, Scotland's reindeer bulls fight for dominance in ruts that can last for hours, and hurricane-strength winds lash a seal colony on Copinsay, where vulnerable pups must stay close to their mother if they are to have a chance of surviving. As winter arrives, the Highlands are blanketed in snow and ice, and while the frigid landscape retains its beauty, the moment of truth arrives for mountain hares, ptarmigan and golden eagles.(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin-Pocock asks astronaut Jessica Meir what it was like to take part in the first all-female spacewalk with Christina Koch.(n)

programme.title: Macbeth: Royal Shakespeare Company
programme.desc: Returning home from battle, the victorious Macbeth meets three witches on the heath. Driven by their disturbing prophecies, he sets out on the path to murder. The 2018 Royal Shakespeare Company production directed by Polly Findlay features Christopher Eccleston as Macbeth and Niamh Cusack as Lady Macbeth.(n)

programme.title: Knights of Classic Drama at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage of some of Britain's most acclaimed acting greats appearing in BBC drama at early stages of their careers. Featuring Michael Caine in a rare Shakespearean role, Ben Kingsley, Ian McKellen, Derek Jacobi and Michael Gambon, as well as behind the scenes footage of famous series including War and Peace, the Mayor of Casterbridge and the Singing Detective. Narrated by David Tennant.(n)

programme.title: British Masters
programme.desc: James Fox explores British art during the years between the two world wars - a period that saw the nation's confidence at a notably low ebb, as the British empire became increasingly fractured. He explores how painters including John Nash, Alfred Munnings, Paul Nash, Stanley Spencer and William Coldstream tried to depict British national identity, and asks what their works reveal about everyday life in the post-war society.(n)

programme.title: A Very British Renaissance
programme.desc: Dr James Fox explores the development of artistic ideas in Elizabethan England in an age obsessed with secrets, with poets writing in code and artworks and buildings concealing mysterious symbols and hidden meanings. The historian examines the portrait miniatures of Nicholas Hilliard, deciphers religious messages hidden in structures designed by Catholic politician Thomas Tresham, and looks at the earliest surviving example of a globe by Emery Molyneux, which symbolised England's appetite to explore the New World.(n)

programme.title: Highlands: Scotland's Wild Heart
programme.sub-title: Autumn and Winter - Against All Odds
programme.desc: Ewan McGregor narrates as summer gives way to autumn and winter in the Highlands. From October to February, extreme weather is expected to batter the Cairngorm plateau. For the region's wildlife, these brutal, dark seasons trigger a game of endurance for which they must carefully prepare. Throughout the autumn, Scotland's reindeer bulls fight for dominance in ruts that can last for hours, and hurricane-strength winds lash a seal colony on Copinsay, where vulnerable pups must stay close to their mother if they are to have a chance of surviving. As winter arrives, the Highlands are blanketed in snow and ice, and while the frigid landscape retains its beauty, the moment of truth arrives for mountain hares, ptarmigan and golden eagles.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Mexico
programme.desc: Documentary about traditional Mexican crafts and those preserving them, beginning by following the making of a loose-fitting tunic known as a huipil.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a purple night-sky scene featuring mountain overlooking a little falling river.(n)

programme.title: Black and British: A Forgotten History
programme.desc: Historian David Olusoga explores the enduring relationship between Britain and people whose origins lie in Africa, looking back over significant people and events throughout history. He visits Cumbria, Eastbourne and Hereford to uncover the stories of generations of people with African descent in Britain, unearthing moments in history that have almost been forgotten. In Eastbourne, David comes face to face with the remains of an ancient, mysterious skeleton nicknamed Beachy Head Woman, with analysis revealing that she was of African descent yet raised in East Sussex.(n)

programme.title: Art of Persia
programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Samira Ahmed takes viewers on a remarkable journey to places rarely seen as she travels through Iran to tell the story of its complex and fascinating people, culture and history. She begins by visiting places rarely seen by Western audiences to reveal how the Persian empire was born, the mysterious writing of its early people and how its powerful kings built a vast realm that became the envy of the ancient world.(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary telling the story of a group of American men and women studying for college degrees while in prison for serious crimes. Shot over four years in maximum and medium security institutions in New York state, Lynn Novick's film tackles the failure to provide meaningful rehabilitation for more than two million Americans living behind bars.(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Concluding part of the documentary about prisoners enrolled in the Bard Prison Initiative, featuring the background stories of participants and their families.(n)

programme.title: Seven Ages of Britain
programme.desc: David Dimbleby studies the art and artefacts that chart England's increasing international influence under Henry VIII and his children, including the wreck of the Mary Rose and Holbein's The Ambassadors. He examines the power of religion with a visit to the Great Bible at Lambeth Palace, and reads the original copies of Shakespeare's history plays at the Globe Theatre.(n)

programme.desc: Show Me What You've Made Of UK kicks off in one of Britain's booming agricultural businesses - an enormous strawberry farm on the south coast which supplies many of the UK's major supermarkets. Our five new recruits may love eating strawberries but are they cut out for serious farm work, getting up at the crack of dawn to go and pick the fruit to strict targets and living on a rural caravan site where the farm's migrant workers set up home for 11 months of the year. programme.title: My Life
programme.sub-title: My Life: The Boy on the Bicycle
programme.desc: A guided tour by 16-year-old Syrian boy, Ahmed, through one of the biggest refugee camps in the world - Zaatari in Jordan. Ahmed is like any other boy his age, except he's had to leave everything behind, including his toys and technology, to move to the refugee camp. Life in Zaatari is nothing compared to home. When the Syrian civil war began in 2012 his family had to abandon their affluent lives and flee across the border to Zaatari camp with thousands of others for safety. Now living in a storage container for a home after his house was destroyed in the civil war, he has lived in Zaatari for three years, along with 80,000 other refugees, who can't leave the camp without permission. Despite their camp having everything they need, from shops to a hospital, school and football pitches, life is very different to how it was before. Ahmed doesn't let this affect his positivity though, and he finds new ways to have fun with his friends - even if it is in a desert sandstorm followed by flood.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: As well as tending to his crops, much of Adam Henson's time on his Cotswold farm is devoted to caring for his animals. Each and every one of them is important to him, from his commercial flock of sheep to his chickens, and from his pigs to his ponies, with many of them having become much-loved characters to the show's viewers. This special edition of Countryfile takes a journey into the archives to revisit and rediscover some of Adam's highs and lows with his marvellous menagerie of creatures.(n)

programme.title: The Salisbury Poisonings
programme.desc: On March 4, 2018, emergency services descend on Salisbury city centre where they find former spy Sergei Skripal and his daughter Yulia unconscious on a park bench. They are taken to hospital where doctors struggle to diagnose the source of their illness, while DS Nick Bailey decides to investigate the Skripals' home for further casualties, but soon after returning to work he too begins to feel ill. Tracy Daszkiewicz, Director of Public Health for Wiltshire Council, is called into an emergency meeting, where it is revealed that it was an attempted assassination with an unidentified poison. Fact-based drama, starring Rafe Spall and Anne-Marie Duff.(n)

programme.desc: The death of George Floyd in Minneapolis has prompted the biggest protests about race and police brutality in the US for 50 years. Around the world, thousands of people have joined marches against racism. Reporter Clive Myrie asks if this could be a moment that changes race relations in America for good. He hears from protesters, eyewitnesses and former police officers about why this killing has had such a powerful impact, and speaks to people from black and white communities in Minneapolis about their hopes and fears for the future.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: As well as tending to his crops, much of Adam Henson's time on his Cotswold farm is devoted to caring for his animals. Each and every one of them is important to him, from his commercial flock of sheep to his chickens, and from his pigs to his ponies, with many of them having become much-loved characters to the show's viewers. This special edition of Countryfile takes a journey into the archives to revisit and rediscover some of Adam's highs and lows with his marvellous menagerie of creatures.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Crathes Castle, Aberdeenshire, where finds brought in to be valued include a collection of signed Harry Potter books, ornate silver from Iona, a German art deco figurine, an intricate Italian bracelet, and a baseball cap with an unusual story to tell.(n)

programme.title: The Salisbury Poisonings
programme.desc: On March 4, 2018, emergency services descend on Salisbury city centre where they find former spy Sergei Skripal and his daughter Yulia unconscious on a park bench. They are taken to hospital where doctors struggle to diagnose the source of their illness, while DS Nick Bailey decides to investigate the Skripals' home for further casualties, but soon after returning to work he too begins to feel ill. Tracy Daszkiewicz, Director of Public Health for Wiltshire Council, is called into an emergency meeting, where it is revealed that it was an attempted assassination with an unidentified poison. Fact-based drama, starring Rafe Spall and Anne-Marie Duff.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: George Floyd: A Killing That Shook the World - Panorama
programme.desc: The death of George Floyd in Minneapolis has prompted the biggest protests about race and police brutality in the US for 50 years. Around the world, thousands of people have joined marches against racism. Reporter Clive Myrie asks if this could be a moment that changes race relations in America for good. He hears from protesters, eyewitnesses and former police officers about why this killing has had such a powerful impact, and speaks to people from black and white communities in Minneapolis about their hopes and fears for the future.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bobby is devastated when Ian tells him that Peter kissed Dotty, while Linda hears how Phil has been treating Sharon and tells Mick she no longer wants to let him have the Vic. Chantelle tries to clear the air with Gray by proving she was at the dentist when he thought she was using his car - but he still thinks she is lying - and Sonia, feeling lonely, decides to search for her long-lost father.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Steph McGovern, Richard Osman, Jennifer Saunders and Joe Sugg join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies.(n)

programme.title: The Salisbury Poisonings
programme.desc: Tracy Daszkiewicz and Supt Dave Minty watch the bodycam footage of DS Bailey walking round the Skripal house and finally make a breakthrough, when they realise he and the Skripals were contaminated by Novichok placed on the front door of the house. As Nick fights for his life, his wife Sarah is beside herself with worry, but tries to remain calm for their daughters. Fact-based drama, starring Rafe Spall and Anne-Marie Duff.(n)

programme.title: I May Destroy You
programme.desc: The action flashes back to three months before the incident as Terry visits Arabella in Ostia, a district of Rome on the Tyrrhenian Sea, where the up-and-coming author is having a whale of a time - all at the expense of her agent. After procuring supplies from local drug dealer Biagio, the pair have a wild night out that takes an unexpected turn for both of them. Drama exploring the question of sexual consent in contemporary life, created by and starring Michaela Coel, with Weruche Opia and Marouane Zotti.(n)

programme.desc: Undercover cameras help to explore the world of animal intelligence, ingenuity and creativity in their hunting, protection and health. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies.(n)

programme.title: The Salisbury Poisonings
programme.desc: Tracy Daszkiewicz and Supt Dave Minty watch the bodycam footage of DS Bailey walking round the Skripal house and finally make a breakthrough, when they realise he and the Skripals were contaminated by Novichok placed on the front door of the house. As Nick fights for his life, his wife Sarah is beside herself with worry, but tries to remain calm for their daughters. Fact-based drama, starring Rafe Spall and Anne-Marie Duff.(n)

programme.title: Pretty Single
programme.desc: Dundonian beauty therapist and single mum Séan strips away her mask of make-up before going on a series of dates with specially selected suitors. Watching on are fellow participants Patricia, Mando, Ali, Michaela and Pamela, who provide supportive, and humorous, commentary throughout.(n)

programme.title: I May Destroy You
programme.desc: The action flashes back to three months before the incident as Terry visits Arabella in Ostia, a district of Rome on the Tyrrhenian Sea, where the up-and-coming author is having a whale of a time - all at the expense of her agent. After procuring supplies from local drug dealer Biagio, the pair have a wild night out that takes an unexpected turn for both of them. Drama exploring the question of sexual consent in contemporary life, created by and starring Michaela Coel, with Weruche Opia and Marouane Zotti.(n)

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer, alongside Maya Jama and Alex Horne, hosts a new variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Tonight, singer-songwriter Liam Gallagher and comedian Rob Beckett join the show for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, and Jamie Redknapp and Kevin Pietersen go head to head in The Sporting Fix. Plus, an exclusive performance in The Garden Sessions from James Bay.(n)

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The coronavirus pandemic has dealt a devastating blow to the performing arts. No one knows when audiences will again be able to pack into a theatre to see a show. So what happens to the writers, performers and venues that enrich our lives? HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to James Graham, the prolific and hugely successful young British playwright whose work has chronicled our turbulent political times. How much do we care about protecting our culture?(n)

programme.desc: Matt Edmondson introduces the records for putting on hair rollers while on a rollercoaster and regurgitating coins, and sees the world's biggest collection of Star Wars memorabilia.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Carla is cornered by a mystery girl in the Rovers and Tim starts to wonder about Geoff's protestations of innocence after Sally tells him she wants Geoff out of the house. Ken fears that Daniel is neglecting himself and Toyah worries about Leanne after she tells her that Oliver has regressed.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates another selection of comical mishaps filmed by viewers. This week, a mechanic uses an airbag as a launch-pad. A moped selfie causes a nasty accident that results in a string of profanities, and a peck from a tropical bird make's Grandad's eyes water. Plus, a video detailing the dangers of wearing a monkey as a wig.(n)

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Dina asks for a demotion so that she is free to date Jonah. Comedy, starring America Ferrera and Ben Feldman.(n)

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Cheyenne begins to go into labour in the store but she cannot stop working as she does not get paid maternity leave, so Jonah proposes a walk out to help her.(n)

programme.title: Dinner Date
programme.desc: Jennifer from Essex picks three blind dates from five potential partners, based entirely on the three course menus they have created. Having dined with the three women, she decides who she wants to see again.(n)

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Dom is hoping to be impressed by the style choices of three potential dates.(n)

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show which takes place in public locations around the UK. He invites passers-by in Glasgow to take part in a series of unusual challenges, with a prize of £1,000 up for grabs.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Carla is cornered by a mystery girl in the Rovers and Tim starts to wonder about Geoff's protestations of innocence after Sally tells him she wants Geoff out of the house. Ken fears that Daniel is neglecting himself and Toyah worries about Leanne after she tells her that Oliver has regressed.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, including a drone flying into a child's face, a unicycling Scotsman and a builder being crushed by a brick wall. Plus, why all members of the Conservative Party have conjunctivitis.(n)

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: The comedian talks to fellow chat show host James Corden. She also video-chats with viral sensations Colt Clark and the Quarantine Kids.(n)

programme.title: Dinner Date
programme.desc: Jennifer from Essex picks three blind dates from five potential partners, based entirely on the three course menus they have created. Having dined with the three women, she decides who she wants to see again.(n)

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Manchester, Hope, Angel and Serena all hope to impress registrar Dominic with their style choices.(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness continues to arrange more dates for his returning panel of 30 single girls. This time, the Love Lift delivers a model from the Wirral, a caricaturist from Blackpool, a student from London, and a musician from Brighton. If any of these men are to secure a chance at love, they must first impress the single ladies enough for them to signal their approval by keeping their lights on.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill celebrates the show's 400th episode, introducing clips of a dog playing the drums and an elephant slipping on a banana skin.(n)

programme.title: Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them (2016)
programme.desc: British author Newt Scamander arrives in 1920s New York with a bagful of magical creatures as part of a global study of fantastic beasts, but gets caught up in a conflict when some of the creatures in his care are accidentally released. With a strange dark force terrorising the city, Newt needs to find some allies to help him round-up the beasts, while saving his own skin in the meantime. Harry Potter prequel, starring Eddie Redmayne, Katherine Waterston and Colin Farrell.(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news.(n)

programme.title: Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them (2016)
programme.desc: British author Newt Scamander arrives in 1920s New York with a bagful of magical creatures as part of a global study of fantastic beasts, but gets caught up in a conflict when some of the creatures in his care are accidentally released. With a strange dark force terrorising the city, Newt needs to find some allies to help him round-up the beasts, while saving his own skin in the meantime. Harry Potter prequel, starring Eddie Redmayne, Katherine Waterston and Colin Farrell.(n)

programme.title: Love Island: Australia
programme.desc: Relationship-based reality show in which singletons seeking a summer of romance compete to pair off in Majorca, in the hope of finding love and winning a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Quagmire falls for Peter's new housemaid and proposes - but his innate bachelor tendencies resurface, and he resorts to desperate measures to get out of marrying her. Meanwhile, Lois tries to wean Stewie off being breast-fed.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The trio move out to allow workmen into the flat, and Robin is more than happy to share a bed - until he finds out who with. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team are in Dorset creating an exciting garden for a young family. His idea for the Drakes is to give them an outdoor space that reflects terminally ill dad Andrew's love of the great outdoors and his passion for working with wood. He opts for a garden with a frontier-land theme that Andrew, wife Rachel and their young son Lucas can enjoy, creating a prairie-inspired back yard of big scale rustic wood structures, agricultural motifs with craftsmanship and the spirit of adventure at its core.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen begins to see the light as she waits to enter her plea, while Sally tells Tim that she is not sure of Geoff's innocence and would like him to move out. Adam quizzes Kelly about her dad and finds out some intriguing information, and Gary does his own investigation work with the help of a PI to try to find Kelly's mum.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The peace of an Oxfordshire village is shattered by the apparently motiveless shooting of a woman through her kitchen window. Twenty-four hours later, her next-door neighbour suffers a similar fate. As Morse searches for a link between the victims, he becomes embroiled in an increasingly bitter election contest at an Oxford college. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Richard Briers, Maggie Steed and John Shrapnel.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Troy is playing in an annual cricket match between Midsomer Worthy and Fletchers Cross when tragedy strikes. The wife of one of the team captains, a wealthy local landowner, is found battered to death with a cricket bat in a quarry. As Barnaby and Troy investigate, they discover no end of people with a grudge against the victim's husband - who may have been complicit in some other suspicious deaths. Guest starring Imelda Staunton and Robert Hardy.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. Arriving to investigate, Poirot delves back into the annals of local life - and uncovers a series of mysterious deaths, any one of which could have a bearing on the present case. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A fox-hunt turns sour when a tramp is found dead in the woods, and matters are further complicated by the murder of the chief suspect's father. As DCI Barnaby investigates, he concludes it is only a matter of time before another member of the tight-knit community suffers the same fate. Guest starring James Bolam.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits his Aunt Alice, who is recovering from an operation at a nursing home, and finds himself in the middle of a mystery. A resident has been found dead and the official verdict is a heart attack, but as rumours of fraud and deception abound, Alice's friend believes someone may have conspired to commit murder.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A barrister is killed with a lethal dose of morphine and suspicion falls on ex-convict Grady Felton, who has recently returned to the village where he committed murder years earlier. However, he has a strong alibi and Barnaby thinks he must be working with an accomplice - until Grady himself is targeted in an arson attack. Detective drama, guest starring Sharon Duce, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the death of a racehorse trainer and suspect an aristocratic family's troubled history holds the key to the mystery - until a second killing makes them think again. Groom Jake Foley seems the obvious suspect, but as events unfold, the detectives' inquiries gradually focus instead on the residents of stately Bantling Hall. Detective drama, starring John Nettles.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted).(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Caroline is opposed to working with Ed Lynch and is furious at what she sees as Danny's betrayal of Du Plessis, whom she plans to reinstall in his rightful place in the business with the help of a familiar face from the past. However, when a bush fire spirals out of control, there are devastating consequences for the entire family. African-set wildlife drama, starring Hayley Mills, Stephen Tompkinson and Deon Stewardson.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective is given a book on Victorian crimes while languishing in hospital, and soon becomes obsessed with a murder committed in 1859. Convinced the three men hanged for the crime were innocent, he sets out to solve the long-forgotten case. Starring John Thaw, Lisa Eichhorn, Judy Loe, Matthew Finney, James Grout and Clare Holman.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Another chance to see John Thaw's farewell appearance as Colin Dexter's Oxford detective. Back from sick leave, Morse uncovers new evidence concerning the year-long investigation into the murder of Yvonne Harrison, but has to cope with playing second fiddle to long-standing sidekick Lewis - and there are questions about whether he is fit enough to handle the case. Kevin Whately co-stars, with James Grout, Jesse Birdsall, Clare Holman and Paul Freeman.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. Arriving to investigate, Poirot delves back into the annals of local life - and uncovers a series of mysterious deaths, any one of which could have a bearing on the present case. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Bentham DeQuetteville falls to his death from a roof after seeing a headless horseman, his aristocratic family seem more concerned about their forthcoming Civil War re-enactment than the incident. As Barnaby and Jones try to discover the truth behind the ghostly figure, their investigation uncovers shocking secrets about the DeQuettevilles. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes, with James Callis, William Gaunt and Raquel Cassidy.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Newlyweds Mike and Ellie decide to build their dream home at a beauty spot called Gypsy's Acre, ignoring the warnings of elusive romany Mrs Lee about an ancient curse supposedly cast upon the land. The couple settle into their new life, but the mysterious woman makes her presence felt, subjecting them to a campaign of hatred in an attempt to force them out. Tragedy soon strikes, but as luck would have it Miss Marple is staying nearby with her recently widowed old friend Mrs Phillpot. However, if the spinster sleuth is to solve the puzzle, she must put her own life in grave danger. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with Wendy Craig, Glynis Barber, Hugh Dennis, Tamzin Outhwaite, Tom Hughes, Joanna Vanderham, Aneurin Barnard, William Hope and Janet Henfrey.(n)

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: Guy is arrested for his attack on Nelson, and Joanne digs deeper into his past. Traumatised by events and still desperate to find Lucinda, he returns home to Kate. Meanwhile, Lisa opens up to Roz about Adam, but Roz is unable to reveal her own secret in return. Lisa and Joe come together to tackle a crisis at the kennels, and assuming that she has Joe's support, Lisa begins to hope for a new future away from her problems with the Rivertys. Starring Anna Friel, Sinead Keenan and Rosalind Eleazar.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Oswald Cooper is the prime suspect in the rape of a teenage girl, but presents an alibi supported by three pillars of the community. When Cooper is murdered, Lewis discovers a web of deceit going back 20 years, in which the dead man was used by the trio to cover up a sordid series of secrets - but the case soon takes on a personal dimension for the inspector. Kwame Kwei-Armah, Sean McGinley, Richard McCabe and Daniela Nardini guest star, with Kevin Whately.(n)

programme.title: The Loch
programme.desc: A human heart is discovered on the shores of the Loch, and forensic psychologist Blake Albrighton is brought in to join the investigating team. He quickly forms a disturbing theory about the motive behind the seemingly inexplicable murders, but Chief Inspector Frank Smilie is quick to dismiss his ideas. However, if the new arrival is correct, the once peaceful Highland community could be set to endure far more deaths, as a serial killer prowls the area. Crime drama, starring Laura Fraser, John Sessions and Don Gilet.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: The family fears that Du Plessis is having a nervous breakdown when he starts to believe he is being haunted by the ghost of Peeters - and the visions drive him to make a confession to Caroline. Danny and Ed come to a rash decision during an animal rescue, endangering everything they have been working toward. African-set wildlife drama, starring Deon Stewardson, Hayley Mills, Stephen Tompkinson and Robert Bathurst.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Another chance to see the feature-length first episode of the popular detective drama. Inspector Jack Frost's investigation into the case of a missing child uncovers a skeleton chained to a locked strongbox that tantalisingly turns out to be empty, but provides evidence of a crime committed 30 years previously. David Jason stars as the irascible detective, with Bruce Alexander, Matt Bardock and Claire Hackett.(n)

programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Reg tells Eric that he won't be taking the job for personal reasons. Liz is upset by the way she and Jim keep annoying each other. Kevin gets a new assistant at the garage.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des is disturbed by his feelings for Liz. Kevin is infuriated by Barry and his passion for punctuality. Jim cooks Liz a special meal to show her he loves her.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris attempts to find out why Kim is back at Home Farm. Susie plans a charity night for the Glovers. Zak and Butch steal a freezer, but there's a shock waiting for them inside it.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: It's Roy's birthday and he announces that he's left school. Frank is determined that Kim will never be allowed to forget what she has done to him.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A mysterious visitor is suspected of involvement in the theft of a priceless Ming vase. Mike tries to discover the reason for Merton's visit to headquarters and Gina starts acting like Aidensfield's answer to Mata Hari. Nostalgic drama, guest starring Leslie Grantham.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George's jealousy rears its ugly head, but Mildred is in no hurry to put his mind at rest.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George's brother discovers documents that prove Mildred married an Army deserter. Comedy, starring Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin's desire to play the guitar proves too much for his flatmates. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When Grace Phillips is killed in an explosion in a munitions factory, Foyle is asked by one of her co-workers to investigate the death. Initially he concludes a tragic accident has occurred until another woman is found murdered and Foyle starts to believe the deaths are connected. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A man who has not been heard of in 20 years and was presumed dead suddenly makes an appearance in Aidensfield - and seems not to have aged a day - prompting Merton to cross swords with an old adversary. Meanwhile, Vernon's attempts to bring a taste of France to North Yorkshire are swiftly ruined as a wine connoisseur dishes the dirt on Scripps' vineyards.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe is confused to find her missing freezer has been mysteriously returned. Kim tries to tell Dave she had no choice but to leave him. Marcus and his friends threaten Scott.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank insists Kim accompanies him to the charity night at the Woolpack, and he taunts Dave at the bar. Terry tries to tell Alan about Jan stealing.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike tells Kevin to find a mechanic or take Barry back. Reg discovers that Eric is going to offer the job to the manager of his Halifax branch and races there to head him off.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alf is happy to help Maud out at the shop as Maureen settles Reg in Lowestoft. Sean questions Des and Liz over their relationship. Jack phones an agency to try out new barmaids.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A petty thief is impaled with a pitchfork in sleepy Midsomer Mallow, jeopardising the residents' chances of winning a 'perfect village' competition. To make matters worse, two of the judges are also killed. Guest starring Timothy West and Hannah Gordon, along with an early appearance by a young Orlando Bloom as the first victim.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A man who has not been heard of in 20 years and was presumed dead suddenly makes an appearance in Aidensfield - and seems not to have aged a day - prompting Merton to cross swords with an old adversary. Meanwhile, Vernon's attempts to bring a taste of France to North Yorkshire are swiftly ruined as a wine connoisseur dishes the dirt on Scripps' vineyards.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: An exciting offer for Edith divides opinion, Isobel throws Ethel a lifeline and Anna's perseverance finally pays off. Daisy is unhappy when Alfred takes a keen interest in newly arrived maid Ivy, while Matthew's concerns about the estate grow - but are his protests falling on deaf ears? Costume drama, starring Laura Carmichael, Penelope Wilton, Amy Nuttall, Joanne Froggatt and Dan Stevens.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse suspects a connection between a woman's disappearance and the unsolved murder of a teenager several years previously. However, the discovery of a body and some unusual elements to the crime scene suggest another possibility. Thursday investigates a lorry hijack which he believes could be the work of a local gangster, and Fancy takes on an informant who is subsequently murdered.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective's god-daughter Lydia and her son James - who is severely traumatised by a bombing at his school - come to stay unexpectedly. Struggling to look after the lad when his mother goes missing, Foyle's life suffers a further complication when the body of a local man is found near a military centre - leading him into the top-secret world of weapons research. Kate Fleetwood and Michael Jayston guest star, with Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective is given a book on Victorian crimes while languishing in hospital, and soon becomes obsessed with a murder committed in 1859. Convinced the three men hanged for the crime were innocent, he sets out to solve the long-forgotten case. Starring John Thaw, Lisa Eichhorn, Judy Loe, Matthew Finney, James Grout and Clare Holman.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Another chance to see John Thaw's farewell appearance as Colin Dexter's Oxford detective. Back from sick leave, Morse uncovers new evidence concerning the year-long investigation into the murder of Yvonne Harrison, but has to cope with playing second fiddle to long-standing sidekick Lewis - and there are questions about whether he is fit enough to handle the case. Kevin Whately co-stars, with James Grout, Jesse Birdsall, Clare Holman and Paul Freeman.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. Arriving to investigate, Poirot delves back into the annals of local life - and uncovers a series of mysterious deaths, any one of which could have a bearing on the present case. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Bentham DeQuetteville falls to his death from a roof after seeing a headless horseman, his aristocratic family seem more concerned about their forthcoming Civil War re-enactment than the incident. As Barnaby and Jones try to discover the truth behind the ghostly figure, their investigation uncovers shocking secrets about the DeQuettevilles. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes, with James Callis, William Gaunt and Raquel Cassidy.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Newlyweds Mike and Ellie decide to build their dream home at a beauty spot called Gypsy's Acre, ignoring the warnings of elusive romany Mrs Lee about an ancient curse supposedly cast upon the land. The couple settle into their new life, but the mysterious woman makes her presence felt, subjecting them to a campaign of hatred in an attempt to force them out. Tragedy soon strikes, but as luck would have it Miss Marple is staying nearby with her recently widowed old friend Mrs Phillpot. However, if the spinster sleuth is to solve the puzzle, she must put her own life in grave danger. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with Wendy Craig, Glynis Barber, Hugh Dennis, Tamzin Outhwaite, Tom Hughes, Joanna Vanderham, Aneurin Barnard, William Hope and Janet Henfrey.(n)

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: Guy is arrested for his attack on Nelson, and Joanne digs deeper into his past. Traumatised by events and still desperate to find Lucinda, he returns home to Kate. Meanwhile, Lisa opens up to Roz about Adam, but Roz is unable to reveal her own secret in return. Lisa and Joe come together to tackle a crisis at the kennels, and assuming that she has Joe's support, Lisa begins to hope for a new future away from her problems with the Rivertys. Starring Anna Friel, Sinead Keenan and Rosalind Eleazar.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Oswald Cooper is the prime suspect in the rape of a teenage girl, but presents an alibi supported by three pillars of the community. When Cooper is murdered, Lewis discovers a web of deceit going back 20 years, in which the dead man was used by the trio to cover up a sordid series of secrets - but the case soon takes on a personal dimension for the inspector. Kwame Kwei-Armah, Sean McGinley, Richard McCabe and Daniela Nardini guest star, with Kevin Whately.(n)

programme.title: The Loch
programme.desc: A human heart is discovered on the shores of the Loch, and forensic psychologist Blake Albrighton is brought in to join the investigating team. He quickly forms a disturbing theory about the motive behind the seemingly inexplicable murders, but Chief Inspector Frank Smilie is quick to dismiss his ideas. However, if the new arrival is correct, the once peaceful Highland community could be set to endure far more deaths, as a serial killer prowls the area. Crime drama, starring Laura Fraser, John Sessions and Don Gilet.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: The family fears that Du Plessis is having a nervous breakdown when he starts to believe he is being haunted by the ghost of Peeters - and the visions drive him to make a confession to Caroline. Danny and Ed come to a rash decision during an animal rescue, endangering everything they have been working toward. African-set wildlife drama, starring Deon Stewardson, Hayley Mills, Stephen Tompkinson and Robert Bathurst.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Another chance to see the feature-length first episode of the popular detective drama. Inspector Jack Frost's investigation into the case of a missing child uncovers a skeleton chained to a locked strongbox that tantalisingly turns out to be empty, but provides evidence of a crime committed 30 years previously. David Jason stars as the irascible detective, with Bruce Alexander, Matt Bardock and Claire Hackett.(n)

programme.desc: Chris attempts to find out why Kim is back at Home Farm. Susie plans a charity night for the Glovers. Zak and Butch steal a freezer, but there's a shock waiting for them inside it.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: It's Roy's birthday and he announces that he's left school. Frank is determined that Kim will never be allowed to forget what she has done to him.(n)
