programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George's jealousy rears its ugly head, but Mildred is in no hurry to put his mind at rest.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George's brother discovers documents that prove Mildred married an Army deserter. Comedy, starring Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin's desire to play the guitar proves too much for his flatmates. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When Grace Phillips is killed in an explosion in a munitions factory, Foyle is asked by one of her co-workers to investigate the death. Initially he concludes a tragic accident has occurred until another woman is found murdered and Foyle starts to believe the deaths are connected. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A man who has not been heard of in 20 years and was presumed dead suddenly makes an appearance in Aidensfield - and seems not to have aged a day - prompting Merton to cross swords with an old adversary. Meanwhile, Vernon's attempts to bring a taste of France to North Yorkshire are swiftly ruined as a wine connoisseur dishes the dirt on Scripps' vineyards.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe is confused to find her missing freezer has been mysteriously returned. Kim tries to tell Dave she had no choice but to leave him. Marcus and his friends threaten Scott.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank insists Kim accompanies him to the charity night at the Woolpack, and he taunts Dave at the bar. Terry tries to tell Alan about Jan stealing.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike tells Kevin to find a mechanic or take Barry back. Reg discovers that Eric is going to offer the job to the manager of his Halifax branch and races there to head him off.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alf is happy to help Maud out at the shop as Maureen settles Reg in Lowestoft. Sean questions Des and Liz over their relationship. Jack phones an agency to try out new barmaids.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A petty thief is impaled with a pitchfork in sleepy Midsomer Mallow, jeopardising the residents' chances of winning a 'perfect village' competition. To make matters worse, two of the judges are also killed. Guest starring Timothy West and Hannah Gordon, along with an early appearance by a young Orlando Bloom as the first victim.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A man who has not been heard of in 20 years and was presumed dead suddenly makes an appearance in Aidensfield - and seems not to have aged a day - prompting Merton to cross swords with an old adversary. Meanwhile, Vernon's attempts to bring a taste of France to North Yorkshire are swiftly ruined as a wine connoisseur dishes the dirt on Scripps' vineyards.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: An exciting offer for Edith divides opinion, Isobel throws Ethel a lifeline and Anna's perseverance finally pays off. Daisy is unhappy when Alfred takes a keen interest in newly arrived maid Ivy, while Matthew's concerns about the estate grow - but are his protests falling on deaf ears? Costume drama, starring Laura Carmichael, Penelope Wilton, Amy Nuttall, Joanne Froggatt and Dan Stevens.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse suspects a connection between a woman's disappearance and the unsolved murder of a teenager several years previously. However, the discovery of a body and some unusual elements to the crime scene suggest another possibility. Thursday investigates a lorry hijack which he believes could be the work of a local gangster, and Fancy takes on an informant who is subsequently murdered.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective's god-daughter Lydia and her son James - who is severely traumatised by a bombing at his school - come to stay unexpectedly. Struggling to look after the lad when his mother goes missing, Foyle's life suffers a further complication when the body of a local man is found near a military centre - leading him into the top-secret world of weapons research. Kate Fleetwood and Michael Jayston guest star, with Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks.(n)

programme.title: World of Sport
programme.sub-title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly
programme.desc: Featuring George Best, Zaire's 1974 World Cup campaign and a selection of own goals.(n)

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Illustrious Client
programme.desc: In the most dangerous assignment of his career, Holmes is asked to stop the marriage of Violet Merville to Baron Gruner - a wealthy collector of objets d'art and beautiful women, whom the sleuth knows to be a murderer. Victorian detective drama, guest starring Anthony Valentine with Jeremy Brett.(n)

programme.title: The Big Fish Off
programme.desc: Team captains Ali Hamidi and Dean Macey continue the competitive angling show, with this edition pitting football pundit Mark Bright against DJ Spoony. A wind-up causes the boys to nearly come to blows in this match that kicks off with an urban challenge in the heart of Walthamstow.(n)

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi, Tom Dove and Neil Spooner head to Italy and travel to a lake in the hills near Bologna, where they have just two days to catch a whopper. Then it is off for the main event at a large water called Viverone, not far from the Alps, where they go up against fish with a reputation for outwitting anglers.(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: Oli Bell and the team look forward to a competitive weekend of racing, plus all the news ahead of Royal Ascot which starts on Tuesday.(n)

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited
programme.desc: Brian Moore presents action from December 1976, including Chelsea v Wolverhampton Wanderers, Leeds United v Aston Villa and Liverpool v Queens Park Rangers.(n)

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: Newcomer Steve Hursey goes on his first winter run - and there is a lot riding on it. His family's sea-to-plate restaurant is low on lobsters stocks and it is his job to re-fill them. He is fishing around Hunter Island, an area known for giant reds and howling westerly winds, but weather is the least of his worries. The rope line to one of his pots is tangled, and it's threatening to pull deckhand Jamie overboard.(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade heads to Russia's remote Amur River, where the mysterious deaths of three men appear to be linked to a freshwater giant - a beast that can exceed 18ft in length and weigh more than a ton. However, he encounters a wall of silence and begins to suspect he has stumbled on to a dark secret that isn't supposed to be uncovered.(n)

programme.title: Football Rivalries
programme.desc: Investigating the intense rivalries between football teams across Europe, focussing on north London sides Arsenal and Spurs.(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present behind-closed-doors coverage of four races from Newbury, including the Diomed Stakes. Plus, three races from Sandown Park. With analysis from Jason Weaver, commentary by Richard Hoiles and reports from Luke Harvey.(n)

programme.title: The Way West (1967)
programme.desc: A senator and a veteran scout lead a band of settlers on an arduous journey across the great plains of 19th-century America. However, they face the prospect of reprisals from Native Americans when one of the pioneers accidentally shoots a child. Western, starring Kirk Douglas, Robert Mitchum, Lola Albright, Michael Witney, Richard Widmark and Sally Field in her big-screen debut.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The Flying Squad is assigned to ensure the safe shipment of a gold bullion consignment, threatened by the plans of a brilliant computer expert bent on revenge. Drama, guest starring George Cole and John Hurt, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

Theo sets out to track down the culprit as the police are made aware of the situation. Detective drama, starring Telly Savalas.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A paparazzi photographer becomes the prime suspect in a jewel theft and the phony kidnapping of a former actress, and helps Theo to bring the real criminals to justice.(n)

programme.title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A reclusive lodger's strange behaviour sends his landlady running to Holmes for advice - and what initially seems to be a simple matter turns into a mystery involving an Italian secret society and murder in New York. Drama, starring Jeremy Brett.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A business deal backfires badly, leaving the future of the Winchester in doubt, so Ray organises a fundraising football match against the police. George Cole and Gary Webster star.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A stakeout in a bowling alley escalates into a full-scale alert when an atom bomb is discovered, primed and ready to detonate. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star in an instalment featuring a fleeting early appearance by Pamela Stephenson.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A new recruit cannot cope with the stresses of the job and puts the lives of other squad members in jeopardy. A young Lesley Dunlop (Emmerdale's Brenda Walker) features alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: The island of Andros in the West Indies boasts tales of swimmers being ripped from the surface of the island's mysterious blue holes by a monster known as the Lusca. Described as half-shark, half-octopus with a taste for human flesh, it is said to drag its prey a thousand feet down to its deep water lair. Extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to the tropical paradise, with his quest for answers taking him to dangerous new depths and closer than ever to a real killer.(n)

programme.desc: In the most dangerous assignment of his career, Holmes is asked to stop the marriage of Violet Merville to Baron Gruner - a wealthy collector of objets d'art and beautiful women, whom the sleuth knows to be a murderer. Victorian detective drama, guest starring Anthony Valentine with Jeremy Brett.(n)

programme.title: The Big Fish Off
programme.desc: Team captains Ali Hamidi and Dean Macey continue the competitive angling show, with this edition pitting football pundit Mark Bright against DJ Spoony. A wind-up causes the boys to nearly come to blows in this match that kicks off with an urban challenge in the heart of Walthamstow.(n)

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi, Tom Dove and Neil Spooner head to Italy and travel to a lake in the hills near Bologna, where they have just two days to catch a whopper. Then it is off for the main event at a large water called Viverone, not far from the Alps, where they go up against fish with a reputation for outwitting anglers.(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: Oli Bell and the team look forward to a competitive weekend of racing, plus all the news ahead of Royal Ascot which starts on Tuesday.(n)

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: Newcomer Steve Hursey goes on his first winter run - and there is a lot riding on it. His family's sea-to-plate restaurant is low on lobsters stocks and it is his job to re-fill them. He is fishing around Hunter Island, an area known for giant reds and howling westerly winds, but weather is the least of his worries. The rope line to one of his pots is tangled, and it's threatening to pull deckhand Jamie overboard.(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade heads to Russia's remote Amur River, where the mysterious deaths of three men appear to be linked to a freshwater giant - a beast that can exceed 18ft in length and weigh more than a ton. However, he encounters a wall of silence and begins to suspect he has stumbled on to a dark secret that isn't supposed to be uncovered.(n)

programme.title: Football Rivalries

programme.title: The Way West (1967)

However, they face the prospect of reprisals from Native Americans when one of the pioneers accidentally shoots a child. Western, starring Kirk Douglas, Robert Mitchum, Lola Albright, Michael Witney, Richard Widmark and Sally Field in her big-screen debut.(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Big Night Out
programme.desc: The story of the night when the future Queen Elizabeth II slipped out of Buckingham Palace with Princess Margaret to party in secret on the streets of London. It was May 8, 1945 - VE Day - the end of the Second World War in Europe. Seventy years later, eyewitnesses who were with the princesses that evening, including the Queen's cousin and close friend Margaret Rhodes and lady-in-waiting Jean Woodroffe, reveal in detail what happened as the pair joined thousands of revellers celebrating peace.(n)

programme.title: The Queen and the Coup
programme.desc: It's February 1953, the first anniversary of Queen Elizabeth's reign. Little does the Queen know she is about to be deployed in a US plot to topple Iran's democratic leader in favour of an all-powerful shah. Planned by MI6, and executed by the CIA, the coup destroyed Iran's democracy and had an impact on relations between Iran and the West. Using declassified secret documents, this documentary reveals the truth of what happened in 1953.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox

programme.title: First Dates Hotel

programme.desc: The broadcaster and journalist reveals the story behind Van Gogh's Sunflowers, visiting the National Gallery to examine the world-renowned painting created by a tormented artist who died when he was just 37 years of age. Andrew tells the barely believable tale of talent, loneliness and tragic heartbreak behind Van Gogh's iconic work, uncovers the clues in it that reveal the darkness that ultimately consumed the man who created it and the part this painting was to play in the tragic story of his death.(n)

programme.desc: In the second of two programmes, Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford meet the who's who of Britain's rich list to work out their secret rules. Jacqueline Gold, the extraordinary force behind Ann Summers, welcomes them into her luxury home to explain the importance of having a revolutionary idea. She also teaches Ruth the ins-and-outs of hosting the perfect sex toy party. Then duo then meet millionaire philanthropist Jack Petchey, still working hard at the age of 91, to learn how much of the secret is simply hard graft. programme.desc: Matt and Garry are in Wolverhampton, where a woman who owes £2,000 in parking fines denies knowing the debtor - despite being a relative. Stewart and Iain confront a woman in Cheshire who is refusing to pay fees to a veterinary practice, claiming her dog has been misdiagnosed, while Paul and Max collect £5,000 owed by a banned company director in east London accused of mis-selling assets on the stock market.(n)

programme.title: 5 News Weekend
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world.(n)

programme.title: Inside
programme.desc: Fiona Phillips examines the changing fortunes of the supermarket chain, which has lost its position at the top of the business in recent years, squeezed between upmarket rivals and discount stores offering lower prices. The programme features contributions from employees, customers and financial experts, and examines the challenges in dominating the middle market and attempting to be competitive on both price and quality.(n)

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Internet entrepreneur Martin and his partner in grime Glenn hear about a huge fly-tipping operation blighting a remote Hampshire beauty spot, and raise a small army of volunteers who roll up their sleeves to sort it themselves. Meanwhile, the UK's network of motorways and A-roads rely upon a system of lay-bys to help keep drivers safe, but these safe pull-in zones also attract an unsightly collection of filthy behaviour, and it falls to Huntingdon lay-by warriors Clive and Steve to take on the speeding traffic, atrocious weather, drunk drivers - and those people who confuse lay-bys with lavatories.(n)

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Single mother Andrea lives in a two-bedroom flat in Lambeth with her three daughters. Over in Chelsea, best friends Geeta and Harriet share a love of the finer things in life - including Geeta's £7million four-storey townhouse. The divorced businesswoman runs a diet clinic and practises what she preaches, with a strict routine of yoga and exercise. How will Andrea, who lives on benefits, find the extravagance of Chelsea living? And how will Geeta and Harriet cope with a quieter life in much more cramped conditions?(n)

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in Blackpool
programme.desc: Documentary following Britons living the high life on a low budget in Blackpool, one of the country's favourite resorts, from holiday-makers to those who have made the Lancashire town their home. Entertainer Simon Green - aka Betty Legs Diamond - talks about his life as a drag queen, and Scottish sisters Agnes and Margaret get more than they bargained for as part of their holiday package. Meanwhile, Jamie Diggins, who works as an estate agent for caravans, takes delivery of a static home that costs an amazing £280,000.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Ever Celeb Embarrassments
programme.desc: Part one of two. Narrated by comedian Chris Ramsey, this collection of quick-fire laughs showcases stars who have ended up with egg on their faces. Red-faced celebrities include Lady Gaga, Will Smith, Rihanna, Beyonce, Justin Bieber and Pink, as well as home-grown favourites Phillip Schofield, Holly Willoughby, Gino D'Acampo, Rylan Clark-Neal and Gemma Collins.(n)

programme.title: Wonderful World of Chocolate
programme.desc: Documentary offering a glimpse inside the confectionery world, visiting factories, chocolatiers and shops, and even revealing the Queen's favourite chocolates.(n)

programme.title: The 21.co.uk Live Casino Show
programme.desc: Interactive gambling.(n)

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi pays a visit to Devon's historic Churston Court. Chris Hodson and Jonathan Smith fell in love with the old building with their hearts rather their heads, and five months in the pair now realise the amount of work needed to bring the hotel into the 21st century. Alex arrives a night early to find a cluttered, dark decor with no one on reception and quickly realises she needs to help motivate Chris to change the way he manages his team. Can she reverse decades of ugly decor, teach her hosts to clear the clutter, erase the oppressive red and herald a more glamorous, curated look?(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: For years, London's East End was denied a new bridge because of the need to guarantee passage of ships up and down the Thames. For years, the greatest engineers of the age hunted for a design that would keep both road and water traffic moving. As Rob Bell explains, the solution came from architect Horace Jones, whose revolutionary design, with its thousand-tonne opening sections, was powered by nothing more than water. Rob also explains the bridge was lucky to survive the Second World War, having been a target for German V-1 flying bombs.(n)

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice and Alistair Appleton look back over highlights from the series. A two-bedroom semi in the Black Country made quite an impression, thanks to the unmistakable smell of dogs and a large collection of dolls, as did a cottage in Northamptonshire that seemed to have four sitting rooms.(n)

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow profiles scientist, engineer, inventor and painter Leonardo da Vinci, whose extraordinary artistic genius gave rise to masterpieces including the Mona Lisa, the Annunciation and the Last Supper. Breaking new ground in portraiture and historical imagery, this giant of the Italian Renaissance used his wide-ranging skills to make a unique contribution to the development of European art.(n)

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: The work of volunteers treating sick, injured and orphaned animals at a wildlife sanctuary.(n)

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: George blows bubbles and Peppa enjoys popping them.(n)

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The playful piglet makes friends with Emily the elephant.(n)

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Granny and Grandpa's pet parrot stays with Peppa, but the bird flies away.(n)

programme.desc: A comet is due to cross the sky, and Mya tries to ensure Dada will able to stay awake this time to see it.(n)

programme.desc: Bulgy thinks people should travel by bus instead of rail, and when he sees an accident at the construction site that pollutes the water tower, he does not tell anyone.(n)

programme.title: Daisy & Ollie
programme.sub-title: How Can I Be Friends with Theo?
programme.desc: The duo meet Uncle Tommy and Daisy's autistic cousin Theo at the aquarium.(n)

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Inspired by Granny Pig's perfume, the piglet experiments with the flowers in her garden to make her own scent.(n)

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The friends try to raise money to fix the school's leaky roof by organising a fete.(n)

programme.title: Ricky Zoom
programme.sub-title: The Most Amazing Things Ever Put in a Box
programme.desc: Loop is helping his dad to deliver a parcel but gets distracted when the parcel splits open.(n)

programme.desc: Becca skips the easy levels of the flying test and goes straight for Level 9 - flying over Cloudy Mountain.(n)

programme.desc: Animated series based on the children's picture books, with the voices of Julian Clary and Jane Horrocks. Great Aunty introduces Princess to the magic of tap dancing.(n)

programme.title: Sunny Bunnies
programme.desc: Hopper tries to get a prize from an arcade machine, but ends up trapped inside it.(n)

programme.title: On Benefits
programme.sub-title: On Benefits: Life on the Dole
programme.desc: People getting by on benefits in the Midlands. Thirty-year-old Derby man James believes his bipolar disorder prevents him having a regular job His dream is to be a full-time entertainer - will his alter ego Mrs Plonker help him into spotlight? Plus, a part-time cleaner who wants to become a model and a former publican whose type 2 diabetes is hindering her search for work.(n)

programme.title: 5 News at Lunchtime
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world.(n)

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Rose must choose between head and heart when an old pal of her late husband turns up and they begin to date, but something stops her from falling for him when he declares his love.(n)

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Artist Lazlo asks Blanche, Rose and Dorothy to pose for him, but the finished sculpture causes an argument.(n)

programme.desc: Australian soap opera charting the latest comings and goings of the residents of Summer Bay.(n)

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Australian soap opera, charting the lives and loves of the residents of the suburb of Erinsborough.(n)

programme.title: Baby Obsession (2018)
programme.desc: Anna is pregnant and excited at the thought of starting a family with her loving husband Jon - unaware that Rachel, whose baby died of cot death years earlier when Anna was babysitting, has been waiting for this day, ready to take her revenge. Thriller, starring Linsey Godfrey and Alicia Leigh Willis.(n)

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross discovers who mugged him as a child and Chandler's internship gets off to an uninspiring start. Meanwhile, Joey endures a draining audition at the hands of a self-important actor. Guest starring Jeff Goldblum and Tenacious D's Kyle Gass.(n)

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Boob Job
programme.desc: Cash-strapped couple Chandler and Monica conceal from each other that they have asked Joey for money. Rachel goes overboard in a bid to make her home baby-friendly, while Phoebe asks Mike to move in with her, but has second thoughts once it becomes clear he does not share her enthusiasm for marriage.(n)

programme.title: 5 News at 5
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world.(n)

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Australian soap opera, charting the lives and loves of the residents of the suburb of Erinsborough.(n)

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Australian soap opera charting the latest comings and goings of the residents of Summer Bay.(n)

programme.title: 5 News Tonight
programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories.(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: A 600-mile journey through one of the most world's most northern countries - Finland - from Helsinki in the south to Kemijärvi in Finnish Lapland. The line whisks passengers through the thickest of forests, past vast frozen lakes 63 times the size of Lake Windermere and to station stops where time has stood still. As the train enters the Arctic Circle, it calls at Rovaniemi, a place destroyed by German soldiers at the end of the Second World War. Now the town has been re-built, famously in the shape of a reindeer, serving as the official home of Santa Claus. Narrated by Bill Nighy.(n)

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A failure to stop in Hartlepool turns into an armed stand-off and a full-blown kidnapping situation, while a 14-year-old boy is found in possession of a bag of crack cocaine, cash and a knife. Lee goes on a high-speed pursuit through Stockton, and Jacko and Simon tackle a drug driver who is wearing a balaclava.(n)

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: A man who fell through a window has a phobia of needles, which leads him to refuse any kind of pain relief and even attempt to discharge himself before his injuries are treated. An aggressive patient is brought in by four police officers after smashing up his mother's home, and has to undergo a toxicology test. Dr Audrey Fox tries to save the life of a patient who has overdosed, and a woman suffers a head injury after slipping on a slice of pizza her husband had left on the floor for their dog to eat.(n)

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Two women are caught up in a driveway dispute with neither backing down, smoke from a wood burner leads one family to take drastic action, and shocking abuse from a neighbour leaves a couple at breaking point.(n)

programme.title: Carrie (2013)
programme.desc: A repressed teenage girl is tormented at school by bullies and at home by her fanatically religious mother. She begins to develop psychic powers and when her prom night results in one final humiliation, she snaps and uses her abilities to wreak a terrible revenge on everyone who has made her life a misery. Horror remake, starring Chloe Grace Moretz and Julianne Moore.(n)

programme.title: Access
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip.(n)

programme.title: Baggage Claim (2013)
programme.desc: An air hostess is horrified at the thought of her younger sister getting married while she is still single. She realises her job gives her access to eligible men from across America, and begins clocking up thousands of air miles as she scours the country for a potential fiance. Romantic comedy, with Paula Patton and Taye Diggs.(n)

programme.title: Shrek Forever After (2010)
programme.desc: The grumpy ogre longs for his simple life as a feared monster, so strikes a deal with magical trickster Rumpelstiltskin to get back to the way things were. However, he soon finds there is a catch - he ends up stuck in an alternate world where none of his friends know who he is and where Rumpelstiltskin has become the kingdom's tyrannical ruler. Animated comedy, with the voices of Mike Myers, Eddie Murphy and Cameron Diaz.(n)

programme.title: Crocodile Dundee (1986)
programme.desc: A New York reporter searches the Australian Outback for a maverick hunter with a legendary reputation, and invites the adventurer back to America. But the die-hard Aussie needs to draw on all his survival skills to adjust to life in the urban jungle. Comedy adventure, written by and starring Paul Hogan, with Linda Kozlowski, John Meillon and Mark Blum.(n)

programme.title: Mother's Day (2016)
programme.desc: As Mother's Day approaches, an array of seemingly unconnected characters all find themselves reconsidering their relationships with their mothers and what motherhood mean to them. Garry Marshall's ensemble comedy in which various families go through dramatic turning points, starring Julia Roberts, Jennifer Aniston, Kate Hudson, Jason Sudeikis, Jennifer Garner and Timothy Olyphant.(n)

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (2014)
programme.desc: A reporter discovers four humanoid amphibians who live in the sewers of New York, and use their martial arts skills to fight crime. She helps them as they face their toughest challenge yet - defeating a sinister criminal organisation that seems to have power over the entire city. Fantasy adventure, starring Megan Fox and William Fichtner, with the voice of Johnny Knoxville.(n)

programme.title: The Shallows (2016)
programme.desc: A surfer strays into the hunting territory of a great white shark, and finds herself stranded on a tiny island a mere 200 yards from the shore. With the deadly aquatic predator between her and the beach and her safe haven slowly disappearing as the tide comes in, she faces a life or death struggle to get safely back to dry land. Thriller, starring Blake Lively and Oscar Jaenada.(n)

programme.title: True Lies (1994)
programme.desc: A seemingly ordinary computer salesman leads a double life as a secret agent battling international terrorism. His naive wife is unaware of his dual employment, and goes in search of adventure - only to end up in the clutches of terrorists who have stolen a nuclear weapon. James Cameron's action comedy, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Jamie Lee Curtis and Tom Arnold.(n)

programme.title: Monsters: Dark Continent (2014)
programme.desc: The Infected Zones have now spread worldwide and giant alien creatures have infested the Middle East. A US military force is deployed to destroy the monsters, as well as dealing with local insurgents. Sci-fi sequel set 10 years after the original film, starring Johnny Harris, Sam Keeley and Joe Dempsie.(n)

programme.title: Sunshine on Leith (2013)
programme.desc: Two soldiers return home to the Scottish port town after finishing their tour of duty in Afghanistan. While they struggle to adjust to life as civilians, the parents of one of the squaddies prepare to celebrate their 30th anniversary in style. Musical featuring songs by the Proclaimers, starring George MacKay, Kevin Guthrie, Peter Mullan and Jane Horrocks.(n)

programme.title: How to Train Your Dragon (2010)
programme.desc: A clumsy Viking boy longs to become a mighty dragon slayer and protect his village from the fearsome flying lizards, but his father thinks he is not up to the task. However, when he sets out to prove his worth, he ends up befriending a dragon, and realises his people may have misjudged the creatures. Animated fantasy, with the voices of Jay Baruchel and Gerard Butler.(n)

programme.title: Crocodile Dundee II (1988)
programme.desc: The rugged Australian adventurer sets out to save his reporter girlfriend from New York drug dealers. Having rescued her from their clutches, he realises they will soon be coming after him - so he decides to even the odds by luring them to the Outback for a final showdown. Comedy adventure sequel, starring Paul Hogan, Linda Kozlowski, John Meillon and Charles S Dutton.(n)

programme.title: Fantastic Four: Rise of the Silver Surfer (2007)
programme.desc: The super-powered team takes on a mysterious alien who has been sent by his planet-eating master to prepare Earth for obliteration. While the four heroes try to find a way to foil its plans, an old enemy returns, intent on seizing the extraterrestrial's power. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Ioan Gruffudd, Michael Chiklis, Chris Evans and Jessica Alba, with the voice of Laurence Fishburne.(n)

programme.title: Evolution (2001)
programme.desc: Two science teachers investigate a mysterious meteor and discover it is oozing a strange fluid containing millions of minuscule but rapidly evolving organisms - which start to turn into terrifying alien monsters with a taste for human flesh. Sci-fi comedy, starring David Duchovny, Orlando Jones, Julianne Moore, Seann William Scott, Dan Aykroyd and Ted Levine.(n)

programme.title: The Gambler (2014)
programme.desc: A literature professor mounts up hundreds of thousands of dollars in debt to a ruthless loan shark and the organiser of an illegal gambling ring after becoming addicted to placing bets. In a last-ditch attempt to pay off his creditors, he risks everything - including his life - on one last game. Thriller, starring Mark Wahlberg, Jessica Lange and John Goodman.(n)

programme.title: Little Fockers (2010)
programme.desc: Nurse Greg is now the proud father of twins, but has yet to earn the respect of his wife's stern father after 10 years of marriage. The couple invite both sides of their extended family to the kids' birthday party, where Greg makes a desperate effort to finally prove his worth to his father-in-law. Comedy sequel, with Ben Stiller, Robert De Niro, Owen Wilson and Dustin Hoffman.(n)

programme.title: Dancer in the Dark (2000)
programme.desc: A poverty-stricken but optimistic woman emigrates from Europe to the US to lead a new life. Stricken with a debilitating optical disease she knows will eventually make her blind, she toils night and day to raise enough money for an operation to spare her son the same fate. But when she is falsely accused of a crime, all her plans for the future are poised to end in tragedy. Lars Von Trier's musical drama, starring Bjork, Catherine Deneuve and Udo Kier.(n)

programme.title: BSL Zone
programme.desc: A young deaf girl learns sign language to try and find herself, but instead she becomes caught between the deaf and hearing worlds.(n)

programme.title: 49th Parallel (1941)
programme.desc: Six Nazis are left stranded in Canada after their U-boat is sunk. As they try to escape the attentions of the authorities, they struggle to win sympathy among the locals. But when that fails, they decide to flee to the still-neutral USA instead. Michael Powell's Oscar-winning Second World War thriller, starring Anton Walbrook, Leslie Howard, Laurence Olivier, Glynis Johns and Niall MacGinnis.(n)

programme.title: Samson and Delilah (1949)
programme.desc: Hebrew warrior Samson becomes a wanted man after crossing paths with Philistine troops, but he constantly thwarts their efforts to capture him using to his incredible strength. Determined to bring him down, the Saran of Gaza makes use of his beguiling sister Delilah, who seduces Samson and manages to rob him of the source of his power. Cecil B DeMille's Oscar-winning adaptation of the biblical tale, starring Victor Mature, Hedy Lamarr, George Sanders, Angela Lansbury and Henry Wilcox.(n)

programme.title: Donovan's Reef (1963)
programme.desc: An American soldier settles down with a friend and a kindly doctor on a South Sea island to relax in the aftermath of the Second World War. The arrival of the medic's snobbish daughter throws their idyllic life into turmoil, leaving them struggling to make her father seem respectable. Comedy drama directed by John Ford, starring John Wayne, Lee Marvin, Jack Warden, Elizabeth Allen and Cesar Romero.(n)

programme.title: X-Men 2 (2003)
programme.desc: Mutant heroes the X-Men are forced into an uneasy alliance with arch-enemy Magneto to battle a common foe. Following an attempt on the US president's life, a sinister military scientist launches a raid on Professor Xavier's school and hatches a plan to rid the world of all mutants. Superhero adventure sequel, starring Hugh Jackman, Patrick Stewart, Ian McKellen, Alan Cumming, Halle Berry and Brian Cox.(n)

programme.title: Logan (2017)
programme.desc: In the near future, Wolverine cares for an ailing Professor X somewhere on the Mexican border. However, the razor-clawed hero's attempts to hide from the world are turned upside down when a young mutant arrives, pursued by lethal forces. Fantasy adventure, starring Hugh Jackman, Patrick Stewart, Dafne Keen and Stephen Merchant.(n)

programme.title: Stoker (2013)
programme.desc: A teenager living an isolated life in her mother's remote estate discovers she has an uncle she has never met following her father's death. The mysterious relative moves into the house and seems to take over his brother's role in the family. His niece suspects he has sinister motives, but becomes dangerously infatuated with him. Thriller, starring Mia Wasikowska, Nicole Kidman and Matthew Goode.(n)

programme.title: Rules of Engagement
programme.desc: Jeff and Audrey are pleased to discover that Brenda is pregnant, but Jeff's hopes of avoiding the surrogate's mood swings are soon dashed. Meanwhile, Jen starts to plan her and Adam's wedding and finds his suggestions unhelpful - but then Timmy steps in to organise the event.(n)

programme.title: Rules of Engagement
programme.desc: Audrey discovers a picture of Jeff at an art gallery and this inspires him to get fit again, while Adam is also taken with the photo and decides to have a professional shoot with Jen. Elsewhere, Audrey encourages Liz to have a good time dating instead of becoming obsessed with finding a potential husband.(n)

programme.title: Rules of Engagement
programme.desc: Jeff and Audrey panic when Brenda reveals that the ultrasound scan suggests they might be having twins. Meanwhile, Russell throws a citizenship party to help Timmy impress a girl.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Mike & Nellie's Steakhouse in Oakhurst, New Jersey, where owner Mike Farber is mourning his father Nellie, who was also his business partner. Having been called in by the restaurateur's two daughters, Gordon faces the task of getting him to focus on his business, before tackling the bigger problem of rotting decor and a less-than-appealing menu.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay travels to the El Greco restaurant in Austin, Texas, where the owners have resorted to using a microwave to prepare their 'authentic' Greek dishes. Gordon faces the difficult task of uniting a squabbling family before tackling the bigger problem of a business on the brink of collapse.(n)

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Laid-back groom Courtney plans to throw a wedding for his controlling bride-to-be Lauren at the caravan park she goes to every year.(n)

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom-to-be Bobby plans a nautical-themed wedding day, but his fiancee Jas dreams of an intimate local wedding.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The precinct's staff prepare for an unusually busy night as the neighbourhood gets into the Halloween spirit, and Amy falls into a particularly foul mood when she is forced to wear a fancy-dress costume during an undercover operation. Meanwhile, Jake bets Captain Holt that he can steal his Medal of Valour before midnight - a challenge that prompts him to don his own selection of disguises.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake makes an arrest without much proof, and so has to spend the next 48 hours collecting enough evidence to keep his suspect in custody. While his co-workers are forced to help him out over the weekend, Charles judges a pie contest between Rosa and Gina, and the Captain gives advice to Terry, who has a problem involving his brother-in-law. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake's dreams come true when he gets the chance to spend the day with his hero, veteran crime reporter Jimmy Brogan, but their encounter has unexpected consequences. Meanwhile, Terry and Charles prepare Rosa for a day in court, teaching her to be less terrifying on the witness stand. Guest starring Stacy Keach.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When Jake's favourite pizzeria burns down, the fire marshal (Patton Oswalt) believes the owner should be the prime suspect in the investigation. Outraged, the detective does everything in his power to prove him wrong, even if it means stepping outside his jurisdiction. Meanwhile, chaos ensues at the precinct after a computer virus publishes everyone's internet browser history.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Santiago hosts a Thanksgiving dinner at her apartment for all her colleagues, but the event risks turning into a disaster, especially when Holt and Peralta have to leave in a hurry to catch a thief who has stolen a large sum of money from the evidence lock-up. Can Boyle save the day?(n)

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: Seven Deadly Sins and a Small Carl Sagan
programme.desc: Mary clashes with Sheldon's drama teacher over plans for a Halloween fundraiser for the church. Georgie falls for a rebellious teenage girl that Sheldon is tutoring.(n)

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: Carbon Dating and a Stuffed Raccoon
programme.desc: The eccentric youngster gets into trouble with fellow child genius Paige at the Science Museum, while George Sr is forced to play therapist to Paige's bickering parents, Barry and Linda. Mary, Georgie and Missy help Meemaw to set up a jumble sale in her garage.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny share an intimate moment as they recover from a psychological deconstruction by the physicist's mother, an expert in psychiatry and neural sciences. Guest starring Mamma Mia! actress Christine Baranski.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Tensions between Sheldon and Penny reach a new high after a paintballing accident leaves an ugly stain on the awkward physicist's favourite sofa cushion. Meanwhile, romance blossoms between Leslie and Howard. Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco star.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard, Sheldon, Howard and Raj discover actress Summer Glau is on their train while travelling to San Francisco. Desperate to discuss the Terminator TV series with her, the friends devise a number of plans to engage her in conversation. With Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons, Simon Helberg and Kunal Nayyar.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny starts her own hair accessories business and the orders start to pile in. However, she makes the mistake of asking Sheldon to help, leading to frustration and regret. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny feels threatened when a sexy actress, who has landed a role in TV series CSI, moves into the apartment building, and gradually begins to sense that her friends are being manipulated by the new arrival. Comedy, starring Kaley Cuoco, Valerie Azlynn, Johnny Galecki and Simon Helberg.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny catches the eye of comic-book shopkeeper Stuart, and Leonard is devastated when she gives serious consideration to dating her new admirer. Desperate for help, Leonard enlists Sheldon to discredit his love rival by preying on his obsession with Batman. Johnny Galecki, Kaley Cuoco and Kevin Sussman star.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Raj take a heartbroken Howard to Las Vegas, where they pay a prostitute to spend time with him. Meanwhile, Sheldon finds himself locked out of his apartment and forced to stay the night at Penny's. With guest star Jodi Lynn O'Keefe (Prison Break).(n)

programme.title: Hulk (2003)
programme.desc: Scientist Dr Bruce Banner turns into the rampaging green Hulk after exposure to a huge dose of gamma radiation, prompting the military to embark on a no-holds-barred mission to destroy the monster. Meanwhile, Banner's unstable father resurfaces, holding the secret to his transformation. Comic-book adventure from director Ang Lee, starring Eric Bana, Jennifer Connelly, Sam Elliott and Nick Nolte.(n)

programme.title: Terminator Genisys (2015)
programme.desc: A soldier fighting in a post-apocalyptic war against living machines is sent back in time to prevent an attempt to change the past - but when he arrives, he finds that history has been changed beyond recognition - and that a plot is being hatched by an unexpected enemy to destroy the future of the human race. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger and Emilia Clarke.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox USA
programme.desc: A rolling cast of famous faces in the US critique the week's TV, including Sharon and Kelly Osbourne, Tyra Banks and Meghan Trainor.(n)

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: The latest diners looking for love are retired labourer Ted, who's 69. He lost his wife Carol in 2013 after 40 years of marriage. Also dining are Fran, 47, who meets 46-year-old builder and part-time erotic thriller writer Colin. programme.title: First Dates

programme.desc: Sheldon wins a grant to pay for a summer expedition to the North Pole, and invites his friends to join him. However, the gang's imminent departure causes Leonard and Penny to reconsider their feelings for each other. Johnny Gale and Kaley Cuoco star.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray's furniture shop faces competition from a rival with an attention-grabbing advertising campaign, so Adam offers to make his father a television commercial starring Marvin. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj does not want Howard to join him in hosting a show at the planetarium. Sheldon goes to great lengths to ensure Amy is free to help him work on a project.(n)

programme.desc: Davina McCall and Rylan Clark-Neal revisit some of the most memorable Big Brother and Celebrity Big Brother episodes as E4 celebrates the show's 20th anniversary.(n)

programme.desc: Ant Middleton organises the recruits into pairs and sets them challenges to test their teamwork - with those who fail to make the grade punished by spending the night outside in a snowhole. programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Property expert Jasmine Harman helps Steph Fessey and Andy Wynn from Hampshire search for a property on Portugal's Silver Coast within a budget of £100,000. The couple are looking for a house with a spacious garden for when their children and grandchildren visit, as well as views of the countryside.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps potential buyers Ali Harris and Steve Ashby to explore the beautiful region of Abruzzo in southern Italy, where the dream of retiring to run a B&B.(n)

In Southampton, Jenny and Tom are keen to move out of their cramped rental property and into a house of their own, but they are facing fierce competition and a fast market. Meanwhile, in the New Forest, Georgie and Will are desperate to live together after seven years of being a couple. However, with Will working in Belgium during the week, the pair are finding suitable properties are snapped up before they have even had the chance to view them.(n)

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Liam Charles and Tom Allen set two chocolate-themed challenges for the four remaining teams in the third episode - and one of the tasks they will face has never been seen before in the show. For the climactic showpiece round, the teams are required to make a firework-themed creation, which must incorporate moving parts as well as featuring chocolate entremets. Judges Benoit Blin and Cherish Finde will assess their efforts before deciding which three teams will be continuing to next week's round.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Training guru Steve Carlyle hosts the first party in York, but a sophisticated evening, complete with a butler, does nothing to boost his reputation as he competes to win the £1,000 prize at the end of the week.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Trevor Rooney, who leads tours of supposedly haunted sites in York, hosts the second dinner party in the city at a bric-a-brac warehouse. He also has several spooky surprises in store for his guests, including a magic show, a paranormal-themed menu and a ventriloquist's doll that spends the evening flirting with the female diners.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The third dinner party in York is hosted by Annie Albericci, who hopes to bring peace, love and harmony to the table with her festival-chic-themed evening. However, things go from bad to worse for Annie when the male guests find a racy photo of her.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nurse Yvonne McGill hosts the fourth dinner challenge in York and goes for a 1960s-themed party, including a game of Snog, Marry, Avoid? and a Beatles tribute band. Meanwhile, guest Steve reveals his celebrity crush.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Foodie Rebecca Ryan hosts the final dinner party in York, and hopes to impress her guests by throwing a Hollywood-themed evening of glitz and glamour, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners of the Sea Verge Hotel in Paignton, Devon, try to prove their B&B offers the best value for money. However, Tony and Chris Fulbrook's guests are far from happy, with Christine Hale complaining about the noise from the sea and scolding Chris for serving breakfast in her slippers.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves on to the Portcullis Hotel in Chipping Sodbury, Gloucestershire, where mother-and-daughter owners Christine and Jenna Hale make a poor first impression by cajoling their guests into taking part in a karaoke session that runs until 2am. Matters worsen the following day, as a caricaturing workshop does little to alleviate the tension among the contestants.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Andy Scott hosts at the Orchid Hotel in Bournemouth, and takes on Richard Bailey in the boxing gym. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table.(n)

programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw restore a classic 1971 Triumph GT6 sports car as owner Andrew can no longer work on it due to he effects of Marfan syndrome.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners of the musically themed Harmony B&B in Torquay, Devon, begin the competition, trying to prove their establishment offers the best value for money. Dutch hosts Hans MacDaniel and Monica van der Meij try to impress their guests, but it remains to be seen what they will make of wheelchair badminton, saxophones at breakfast and a controversial sausage.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest continues at Chimneys B&B in North Walsham, Norfolk, where owners Jenny and Richard Harmer's decision to use a flag as a tablecloth angers Hans MacDaniel and Monica Van Der Meij. The argument seems to abate during an afternoon canoeing session - only to resurface at breakfast the following day.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Beverley Conoley and Caroline Pocklington host at the Wordsworth Hotel in Cockermouth, Cumbria. They take their guests to a spa and Hans MacDaniel happily volunteers for a spray tan, while Richard Harmer is far more uncomfortable as he finds himself on a massage table. The day ends with dinner at a nearby bistro, but Jenny Harmer complains about the temperature.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mother and daughter Lesley and Emily Gray play host at the Park Farm Guest House in Gateshead, but while the evening's entertainment featuring a ride on a two-wheeled personal transporter goes down well among the guests, tensions simmer when the dinner conversation turns to a delicate issue.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Simran Samra hosts the first party in east London, and decides to cook meat for her main course, despite being a vegetarian. The fashionista tries to salvage her chances of winning the £1,000 prize by performing a range of comedy accents after fellow competitor Alan Price upsets one of the guests.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Comedian Yoshki Greenberg hosts the second dinner party in east London, but her decision to surprise the guests by asking them to wear animal-themed pyjama suits does not go down well - leaving her struggling to win them over with a stand-up routine.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Alan Price hosts the third party in east London, and sets out to impress his fellow competitors with a medieval-themed dinner. He hopes to raise the standard of the contest with an ambitious menu, but stumbles at the first hurdle when vegetarian diner Simran Samra panics at the sight of his authentic pig's head as the table centrepiece. Things do not improve as the night goes on, with dirty dinner plates and sloppy hosting leaving the guests less than impressed.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Bar owner Delia Elliot hosts the fourth dinner party from east London, and she is confident she can teach her young diners the protocol of entertaining guests. However, prankster Alan Price ends up having an argument with her, leading Simran Samra to take evasive action.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Doctor Chintu Gademsetty hosts the final dinner party in east London, and is confident of impressing his guests with a 1980s-themed evening. Even though his dance competition is not a hit with all of the diners, his food goes down a treat, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud oversees the efforts of Jonathan Belsey, a scientist who is trying to build a carbon-neutral home for his partner and their four daughters. The design and construction are risky because they feature untested technology and an uncompromising choice of materials, and he soon finds the costs spiralling out of control.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A paramedic heads to a serious traffic collision where a truck has overturned, while others deal with one of the most challenging emergencies - a cardiac arrest in the street.(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Wedding
programme.desc: The marriage between Elizabeth II and Prince Philip has stood the test of time and helped keep the monarchy secure. This programme captures the story of the Queen, whose love life as a princess became a matter of political significance, recalling how Prince Philip's suitability was called into question and the role played by his uncle Louis Mountbatten in the young couple's relationship.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Marie, 31, arrives at St George's A&E in south-west London by ambulance after collapsing at home with an unexplained headache. Doctors work fast to ascertain the cause of her collapse, with potentially devastating results. Marie's joined by her partner Cameron and her young child. Cameras also follow Eileen, 87, who comes in with shortness of breath and a high heart rate, and 10-month-old Erin, who has a build-up of wax in her ears following a cold and fever.(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: A woman who has been badly cut in a Saturday night pub fight endures an agonising wait for medical care, while medics attend a habitual caller who has requested help rather than use medication to control his symptoms - one of many non-urgent cases handled by the Wiltshire's.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef visits beachside restaurant Barefoot Bob's in Hull, Massachusetts, owned by husband-and-wife team Marc and Lisa. Every year profits nosedive through the bleak winter months, and matters aren't improved when it becomes clear they have both lost their passion - not only for the business but also for each other. With the restaurant and marriage on the line, Gordon tries to help turn things around before it's too late.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Marie, 31, arrives at St George's A&E in south-west London by ambulance after collapsing at home with an unexplained headache. Doctors work fast to ascertain the cause of her collapse, with potentially devastating results. Marie's joined by her partner Cameron and her young child. Cameras also follow Eileen, 87, who comes in with shortness of breath and a high heart rate, and 10-month-old Erin, who has a build-up of wax in her ears following a cold and fever.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The priests organise a raffle to raise funds to repair their leaky roof, planning to award a car as the prize. Unfortunately, Ted accidentally wrecks it before the grand draw takes place, and embarks on a frantic search for a replacement. Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon play the Craggy Island clerics.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp demonstrates how to create handmade and personalised gifts. She learns about sugarcraft and perfume-making, and also receives a lesson in the art of designing Victorian-style silhouettes.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Cheshire mother of four Helen Broomhead look for a property in the Cinque Terre region of Italy where she can escape from the pressures of day-to-day life.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien repurposes an old butcher's block into a coffee table, while Henry Cole restores a pair of go-karts to race against restorer Guy.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole refurbishes a classic Ford Mustang and a neon garage sign, while Simon O'Brien restores a classic Chopper bicycle and turns a bass guitar into a chair.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree only have a few weeks to prepare for their wedding, but the chateau is nowhere near ready to receive the 200 guests they have invited and finding reliable, affordable workers is proving difficult. Dick oversees the damming of the moat and the installation of a new, eco-friendly sewage system, while Angel turns her attention to giving the rooms a distinctive decor.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets people as they bid to turn an empty plot of land into their dream home on an affordable budget. Adam and Vicki Nicholl have become tired of city life and decide to move back to the Hertfordshire village of Pirton where they grew up to build a barn-style property in the garden of Adam's mum's home. The pair are convinced they can save around £100,000 simply by not purchasing an existing property.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off in Worcestershire, where friends Geri Atterbury and Lorraine Rigby invite guests to stay at Greenways of Malvern. On arrival, couple Sean McGahern and Maxine Parker are not impressed by the wild wallpaper, and the lack of an en-suite dismays mother-and-son duo Sarah and Luke Allaway.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Royal Forester at Bewdley in Worcestershire for their second stay, with proprietors Sean McGahern and Maxine Parker hoping to impress their guests with a trip to a local safari park. Sarah and Luke are impressed with their colourful room, but Sanjeev and Elinor have a 'hairy' experience.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: From Ferry Lodge in Holyhead, Anglesey, where mother-and-son duo Sarah and Luke Allaway hope to impress the other contestants. However, things do not get off to a good start when Geri and Lorraine are overwhelmed by the number of laminated signs, Sean and Maxine begrudge paying 50p for toiletries and Sanjeev and Elinor are left unimpressed by the cleanliness of the establishment.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Partners Sanjeev Bhagotra and Elinor Roberts welcome their guests to Court Colman Manor, Bridgend, Glamorgan. Sean McGahern and Maxine Parker are pleased with the size of their room on arrival, but Sarah Allaway discovers a collection of cobwebs in hers. During the afternoon, the groups take a lesson in fire-making, and the next morning a traditional Welsh breakfast is not to everyone's taste - with the hosts wondering whether tactical voting has played a part come feedback time.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet one last time to find out what they have been paid. Geri kicks off proceedings by demanding to know why Sarah and Luke did not want a return visit to Greenways of Malvern, before they in turn confront Sanjeev and Elinor over their low cleanliness score for Ferry Lodge. Then the Court Colman Manor proprietors accuse Sarah and Luke of unfairly judging their hotel, before the payments are revealed and the winners are announced.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole head to Surrey and Hampshire. Simon builds a huge kitchen table from discarded rafters and gives a 1970s campervan a new lease of life. Henry hankers after an American tractor and Royal Enfield motorbike and sidecar.(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales and the Lakes
programme.desc: Cameras follow people who live and work in the national parks, beginning with the residents of Bleak Bank Farm in the Dales.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree make plans to host weddings in the grounds of their properyt, while looking ahead to keeping livestock and growing their own crops. Dick takes steps to generate solar power during a hot summer, and turns an old oil tank into a pig roaster.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows interior designer Kathryn Tyler as she tries to create a Scandinavian-style eco house in her mother's back garden in Falmouth, Cornwall, close by the Lizard Peninsula. The plan is ambitious, featuring a white-painted brick ground floor, a lightweight timber-frame first floor and cleverly designed spaces, but the small size of the plot and Kathryn's inexperience could hamper the project.(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales and the Lakes
programme.sub-title: The Yorkshire Dales and the Lakes: Farming Lives
programme.desc: A special edition celebrating the farming heritage in the two national parks, including champion sheep breeder Arnold Lancaster preparing his flock for the Westmorland County Show.(n)

programme.title: Nazi Megastructures
programme.desc: A look at Japan's surprise air attack on Pearl Harbour, revealing how it required months of secret preparation, innovations in torpedo design, and clever counter-intelligence.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, with captain Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson taking on guest captain Kevin Bridges and Jessica Knappett. John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Steve Backshall's Extreme River Challenge
programme.desc: The naturalist travels down the Baliem River in New Guinea from its source to the sea, hoping to encounter the ancient tribes and dangerous wildlife that live long its banks and in the surrounding jungle. He begins his journey inland at Lake Habbema, and braves some of the world's fiercest white water rapids. Along the way, he stays in a village belonging to the Dani tribe and ventures into some previously unexplored caves.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Matt LeBlanc and Chris Harris review the new Bentley Continental GT, while Chris also attempts to tame the scariest Porsche 911 of all time. Rory Reid test drives the new Renault Megane hot hatch, and Gregory Porter joins the team in the studio.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programme.desc: The chef continues his gastronomic road trip from Venice to Istanbul taking in the countries of the former Byzantine Empire - a renowned melting pot of East and West. This instalment finds the gastronome in the city of Ravenna, where he discovers Piadinas, a thin Italian flatbread street food dating back to Roman times. He then crosses the Adriatic Sea to Croatia - a first for Rick - and a gets a chance to discover new a wealth of dishes such as pasticada, goat stew with peas and lamb peka.(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank stumble on a surprise find in a man-cave full of cars. They also encounter an eccentric seller who answers to an otherworldly power.(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank are feeling lucky as they head to the huge stockpile of a Kentucky collector who is desperate to sell his collection before his roof caves in.(n)

programme.desc: Brandon and Lori discover a dead cow in one of their storage unit lots.(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines the social significance of cars in the 20th century. He begins by travelling to Germany, Italy and Russia to examine how dictators kick-started the mobilization of the masses, finding tales of design brilliance, abject failure, war, fraud and double dealing. James also learns about how the British motor industry blew a gift-wrapped chance to rule the world and he gets his own back with a stunt that means bad news for one of the planet's most hated cars.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are in Russia as the motoring magazine returns, taking part in a race across the urban landscape of St Petersburg. James defends the honour of the car in a very small Renault, Richard tries to prove that pedalling is faster by riding a bicycle, while Jeremy attempts to demonstrate that water is the quickest way across town by piloting a hovercraft. To make matters more complicated, all three presenters are racing not only one another but also the Stig, who is making his way toward the finish line by public transport. Plus, the new Lamborghini Huracan is put through its paces and singer-songwriter Ed Sheeran takes to the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May work on a project to build mobility scooters capable of tackling the British countryside's terrain. Ferrari's four-wheel-drive FF is pitted against the revised, V8-engined Bentley Continental GT on a test track at the edge of the Arctic Circle, while James puts the Fisker Karma through its paces in Florida, and meets AC/DC frontman Brian Johnson.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May pay tribute to Saab, the Swedish car manufacturer that created several successful models since the late 1940s, but has now ceased production. Richard Hammond takes the passenger's seat in a Skoda rally car that races a rocket-propelled flying man, and actor Matt Smith gets behind the wheel of the Reasonably Priced Car. Jeremy also shreds the tyres of the noisy Maserati GranTurismo MC Stradale and the 510bhp Mercedes-Benz C63 AMG Black Series.(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The series reaches its majestic climax. Who will win Greg Davies's coveted golden head to become just the seventh Taskmaster Champion in history?(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner chairs the comedy panel show, inviting comedians Lee Mack and Ruby Wax and Hairy Biker Dave Myers to compete to have their pet hates banished for ever. Their gripes include Top Gear, new-age books and chunky chips.(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: TV presenter Vernon Kay, interior designer and Dragons' Den entrepreneur Kelly Hoppen, and comedian and actor Miles Jupp compete to have their pet hates banished on the lively panel show chaired by Frank Skinner. Their gripes include litter louts, toilet-roll covers and loud noises.(n)

programme.desc: Kevin Bridges hosts the stand-up show from the Hammersmith Apollo in London, warming up the audience before introducing routines by DJ, Buzzcocks regular and all-round funnyman Phill Jupitus and actress-comedienne Sara Pascoe, making her Apollo debut.(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition. programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the unusual ways in which flamingoes and blue whales eat, examining the ways feeding patterns have influenced both species' behaviour in similar ways.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores animals that have developed tool-making skills to help them survive, including orangutans and crows.(n)

programme.desc: Rare footage of the elusive animals that live in the unforgiving environment of the world's mountain ranges. A female snow leopard with a cub ends up caught between two rival males, grizzly bears rub themselves up against trees in an attempt to shed their winter fur, and golden eagles hunt over snow-capped peaks.(n)

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: Documentary following the fortunes of humans and animals over the course of a year in Alaska, which is by far the biggest US state and still one of the wildest places on Earth with deep forests and vast mountain ranges. The arrival of spring sees Alaska go through a huge transformation as water, light and warmth return, with animals emerging from hibernation and rushing to cash in on the riches of the season. Dougray Scott narrates.(n)

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: The land is bathed in 24-hour sunlight during the short Alaskan summer, luring hummingbirds up from Mexico, exposing deserts in the Arctic and relying on parachuting firefighters to tackle forest fires in its vast wilderness. Grizzly bears fatten up on a sudden wealth of salmon, a caribou calf joins a vast herd on its great migration and a huge male moose finds unlikely ways to impress a female. Among the human population, a dedicated grower of giant vegetables takes his half-ton pumpkin to the Alaska State Fair.(n)

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: To survive the bone-chilling cold, deadly blizzards and darkness of the eight months of an Alaskan winter takes courage, cunning and remarkable endurance. This episode tells the stories of how the tough and resourceful animal and human inhabitants of the windswept mountain peaks, icy tundra and snowbound forests face up to the challenges of the season.(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Creatures that live in volcanic hot spots on the ocean bed, areas that reach temperatures hot enough to melt lead and which may hold the secrets behind all life on Earth. Other strange creatures from the depths of the sea include bone-eating worms, fish that walk rather than swim, and hordes of squid. Plus, footage of sharks in a feeding frenzy over the dead body of a whale and the wildlife of the Midnight Zone in the Pacific, an area that never receives any natural light, forcing creatures to rely on their own bioluminescence.(n)

Crime drama, directed by and starring Patrick McGoohan, with Peter Falk and Leslie Nielsen.(n)

Crime drama, starring Peter Falk and Robert Vaughn, directed by Patrick McGoohan.(n)

Crime drama, guest starring Peter Coyote, with Vincent D'Onofrio.(n)

programme.desc: Documentary following the work of New Zealand police officers as they try to keep motorists safe on some of the country's most dangerous roads.(n)

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Renowned dog trainer Cesar Millan gets tough with an overweight dog that refuses to leave its chair, and a rescue dog that is afraid of men.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: The Great Dragon is finally free, and attacks Camelot. With the castle crumbling, the knights cannot hold out much longer - only a Dragonlord can kill such a beast and Uther wiped them all out years ago, or so it is believed. Gaius reveals one such man may still be alive and in hiding, so Arthur and Merlin set out in search of him, before the kingdom is destroyed. But even if they find him, can they persuade him to help his old enemy Uther? Colin Morgan, Bradley James, Richard Wilson and Anthony Head star, with a guest appearance by John Lynch.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.sub-title: The Tears of Uther Pendragon - Part One
programme.desc: A year after her disappearance, Morgana returns to Camelot, where a delighted Uther immediately announces a banquet in her honour. However, when evil magic begins to take hold of the king's mind, Merlin suspects the lady is hiding a sinister secret, only for the wizard's search for the truth to leave him at the mercy of the scorpion-like enchanted serkets. Colin Morgan and Katie McGrath star.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.sub-title: The Tears of Uther Pendragon - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Following continued manipulation from Morgause, Cenred launches an attack on the citadel that places the fate of Camelot on Arthur's shoulders - but the initially strong counter-attack is overwhelmed when Morgana uses the enchanted rowan staff given to her by Morgause to summon a skeleton army from the vaults beneath the castle. Merlin hopes to put an end to the battle by stopping Morgana and vanquishing the enemy within - but must achieve his goal without revealing his magical powers. Adventure, starring Colin Morgan and Bradley James.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: After stumbling across a secret chamber, the young wizard accidentally releases a goblin - and there is chaos in Camelot when the mischievous creature possesses Gaius. Uther suddenly turns bald and the royal family starts suffering from terrible bouts of flatulence. With the monster running riot, Merlin races to solve the problem before any more damage is done. Colin Morgan and Richard Wilson star, with The Fast Show's Mark Williams as the voice of the goblin.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: An enigmatic man steps in to help Merlin and Arthur when they find themselves outnumbered in a tavern brawl. Risking his life to protect the prince, the stranger is seriously wounded and taken back to Camelot, where he quickly makes a recovery and begins drinking, charming the ladies and causing trouble for Merlin. Guest starring Eoin Macken.(n)

programme.title: Blood & Treasure
programme.desc: On the trail of Cleopatra's body, Danny and Lexi pose as a couple and travel to a castle previously used by the Nazis. Matt Barr and Sofia Pernas star.(n)

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: A Canadian woman tests positive for heroin, customs officials suspect a Malaysian tourist is illegally looking for work, and something sinister is found inside a child's laptop.(n)

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: A man tries to get a bag of meat through customs and would rather go to court than pay the fine, and a suspicious woman attracts the attention of security. Elsewhere, an Indian student has been skipping school and faces being sent home.(n)

programme.title: Hawaii Five-0
programme.sub-title: 'Ka Lala Kaukonakona Haki 'Ole I Ka Pa A Ka Makani Kona (The Tough Branch That Does Not Break in the Kona Gale)
programme.desc: Steve joins Junior and his SEAL team on a mission to capture an elusive person who is holding his mentor hostage. Starring Alex O'Loughlin and Scott Caan.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: The wizard has an unexpected meeting with mysterious old sorcerer Taliesin, who takes him to the crystal cave and shows him a glimpse of the future. However, the gift turns out to be more of a curse when Merlin sees Morgana preparing to kill Uther and realises he faces the dangerous task of trying to change the course of destiny. Magical adventure series, starring Colin Morgan.(n)

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A look at the life of Polish serial killer Karol Kot, nicknamed the Vampire of Krakow for his wildly varying choice of victims, from young children to the elderly.(n)

programme.title: Fred Dinenage: Murder Casebook
programme.desc: The presenter explores the case of John Straffen, who killed two young girls in the summer of 1951. He was found to be unfit to plead and committed to Broadmoor Hospital, but during a brief escape from the facility in 1952 he killed again. It was determined that the cause of his behaviour was severe brain damage, traced back to an attack of encephalitis as a child in India. When he died in 2007, he was the longest-serving prisoner in Britain.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: A murder trial drags on for seven weeks, causing tempers to fray among the jury. Eventually the only dissenting member is found dead - and suspicion inevitably falls on the other 11. Meanwhile, Stokes reopens an old case on the basis of fresh evidence provided by the victim's sister. Drama, starring William Petersen and George Eads.(n)

programme.desc: A boy living in an orphanage grows up to be the institution's lovable but clumsy caretaker. He must save the children's home from a wicked property developer who wants to build a factory on the site, and tricks him into taking the kids to the seaside so he can knock the building down. Comedy, starring Norman Wisdom and Joan Rice.(n)
