programme.desc: A shy widower gets entangled in romantic intrigue while rehearsing with an amateur theatrical troupe in Scarborough. Comedy based on Alan Ayckbourn's stage play, starring Jeremy Irons, Anthony Hopkins, Prunella Scales, Lionel Jeffries, Jenny Seagrove, Gareth Hunt and Patsy Kensit.(n)

programme.desc: A British army officer searches for a Jewish refugee in post-war Germany, only to discover that she has lost her memory. Romantic mystery, starring Robert Beatty and Mai Zetterling.(n)

programme.desc: Veterinary surgeon Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Labrador whose cancer is eating away its front leg, by replacing its tumour with a custom-made implant, and uses the same technique to save the knee of a border collie. Senior surgeon Michael Hamilton also tends a chinchilla that needs urgent treatment on its arm bones after falling from a shelf.(n)

programme.title: Oxford Street Revealed
programme.desc: Police instinct brings about a lucky break when the anti-theft team cracks down on pickpockets. The paparazzi and celebrities are out for a charity launch, a tourist takes a nasty fall on the Tube, and two new restaurants are opening simultaneously at a department store, presenting technological challenges for staff. Also featuring the Hare Krishnas dedicated to bringing a little spirituality to the world's most famous shopping street.(n)

programme.title: The Repo Man
programme.desc: In recession-hit Britain, many people have turned to finance companies to purchase items over a fixed period of time. This documentary follows the life and work of bailiff Sean James as he confronts debtors across the nation who fail to keep up their repayments.(n)

programme.title: Dying of the Light (2014)
programme.desc: A CIA agent is diagnosed with a degenerative condition that results in him being forced to retire from the world of intelligence. When he receives word that a terrorist who once tortured him has resurfaced, he goes rogue and sets out to assassinate the man while he is still able. Thriller, starring Nicolas Cage, Anton Yelchin and Irene Jacob.(n)

programme.title: London Town (2016)

programme.title: Caravaggio (1986)
programme.desc: The Renaissance artist lies on his deathbed and remembers his tumultuous life. As his revolutionary approach to religious art raises controversy in the Catholic Church, the painter becomes infatuated with a married brawler, leading to a destructive love triangle. Derek Jarman's biopic, starring Nigel Terry, Sean Bean, Michael Gough and Tilda Swinton in her movie debut.(n)

Second World War adventure, starring Peter Finch, Tony Britton and Eva Bartok.(n)

programme.title: Very Important Person (1961)
programme.desc: A pompous Allied scientist ends up in a PoW camp after crash-landing during a reconnaissance mission over Germany. His uncompromising attitude and a series of misunderstandings initially earn him the enmity of his fellow inmates, but his popularity improves when he devises an audacious escape plan. Second World War comedy, with James Robertson Justice, Stanley Baxter, Leslie Phillips and Eric Sykes.(n)

programme.desc: Officers of MI5 and Special branch join forces to deal with a wave of sabotage attacks that have been sweeping the nation. Spy thriller, starring Liam Redmond and Andre Morell.(n)

programme.title: Capital Conversation
programme.desc: London-based current affair series, with Michael Hayman.(n)

programme.title: Meet the Lords
programme.desc: The Government threatens to trim the Lords' powers if they rebel again and Lord Strathclyde publishes his review into the powers the upper house have to block legislation. Cameras follow Labour peer Alf Dubs as he attempts to push through an amendment to the Immigration Bill to allow unaccompanied refugee children into the country, while Admiral Alan West isn't happy about a public poll that has voted overwhelmingly to name a new polar research ship Boaty McBoatface and raises a question about it.(n)

programme.title: Callan

programme.title: Golden Years (2016)
programme.desc: A retired couple face a grim future after a financial crisis costs them both their pensions. Refusing to take the situation lying down, they resolve to take back what is rightfully theirs with a series of daring bank robberies. Comedy, starring Bernard Hill, Virginia McKenna, Simon Callow and Sue Johnston.(n)

programme.title: The Face of Love (2013)
programme.desc: A widow begins a new relationship with a man who is the exact double of her husband, and shares many of his personality traits. Over time, she grows to love his own unique qualities, but fears he will leave her if he ever discovers the resemblance. Romantic drama, starring Annette Bening, Ed Harris and Robin Williams.(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner.(n)

While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner.(n)

programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. Each duo are allowed to determine how many spaces they would like to move by answering the corresponding number of questions. Landing on a space reveals it's hidden contents, which could amount to a prize, or a 'hot spot', which causes contestants to forfeit their gathered winnings.(n)
